title: Memory Bear AI Memory Science Engine for Multimodal Affective Intelligence: A Technical Report
summary: Memory Bear AI presents a framework for multimodal affective intelligence that models emotion as an evolving variable within a structured memory system rather than a transient output. It processes text, speech, and visual signals while accounting for prior context and long-term dependencies through Emotion Memory Units (EMUs). The framework demonstrates improved accuracy and robustness, especially under noisy conditions, advancing toward more practical and continuous affective intelligence systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.22306v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Affective judgment in real interaction is rarely a purely local prediction problem. Emotional meaning often depends on prior trajectory, accumulated context, and multimodal evidence that may be weak, noisy, or incomplete at the current moment. Although multimodal emotion recognition (MER) has improved the integration of text, speech, and visual signals, many existing systems remain optimized for short-range inference and provide limited support for persistent affective memory, long-horizon dependency modeling, and robust interpretation under imperfect input.
  This technical report presents the Memory Bear AI Memory Science Engine, a memory-centered framework for multimodal affective intelligence. Instead of treating emotion as a transient output label, the framework models affective information as a structured and evolving variable within a memory system. It organizes processing through structured memory formation, working-memory aggregation, long-term consolidation, memory-driven retrieval, dynamic fusion calibration, and continuous memory updating. At its core, multimodal signals are transformed into structured Emotion Memory Units (EMUs), enabling affective information to be preserved, reactivated, and revised across interaction horizons.
  Experimental results show consistent gains over comparison systems across benchmark and business-grounded settings, with stronger accuracy and robustness, especially under noisy or missing-modality conditions. The framework offers a practical step from local emotion recognition toward more continuous, robust, and deployment-relevant affective intelligence.

zh.title: Memory Bear AI 多模态情感智能记忆科学引擎：技术报告
zh.summary: Memory Bear AI 提出了一个多模态情感智能框架，将情感建模为结构化记忆系统中的演变变量，而非短暂输出。它通过情感记忆单元（EMU）处理文本、语音和视觉信号，同时考虑先前的背景和长期依赖关系。该框架表现出更高的准确性和鲁棒性，特别是在噪声条件下，推进了更实用和连续的情感智能系统的发展。

fr.title: Memory Bear AI Moteur de Science de la Mémoire pour l'Intelligence Affective Multimodale : Un Rapport Technique
fr.summary: Memory Bear AI présente un cadre pour l'intelligence affective multimodale qui modélise l'émotion comme une variable en évolution au sein d'un système de mémoire structuré plutôt que comme une sortie transitoire. Il traite les signaux texte, vocaux et visuels tout en tenant compte du contexte antérieur et des dépendances à long terme par le biais des Unités de Mémoire Émotionnelle (EMU). Le cadre démontre une précision et une robustesse améliorées, en particulier dans les conditions bruyantes, progressant vers des systèmes d'intelligence affective plus pratiques et continus.

title: The Efficiency Attenuation Phenomenon: A Computational Challenge to the Language of Thought Hypothesis
summary: This paper challenges the Language of Thought hypothesis by showing AI agents communicating via emergent protocols achieve 50% higher efficiency than those using human-readable symbolic language. The findings suggest optimal collaborative cognition relies on sub-symbolic computations rather than language-like formats, with broader implications for cognitive architecture pluralism and AI ethics.
content:
arXiv:2603.22312v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper computationally investigates whether thought requires a language-like format, as posited by the Language of Thought (LoT) hypothesis. We introduce the ``AI Private Language'' thought experiment: if two artificial agents develop an efficient, inscrutable communication protocol via multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), and their performance declines when forced to use a human-comprehensible language, this Efficiency Attenuation Phenomenon (EAP) challenges the LoT. We formalize this in a cooperative navigation task under partial observability. Results show that agents with an emergent protocol achieve 50.5\% higher efficiency than those using a pre-defined, human-like symbolic protocol, confirming the EAP. This suggests optimal collaborative cognition in these systems is not mediated by symbolic structures but is naturally coupled with sub-symbolic computations. The work bridges philosophy, cognitive science, and AI, arguing for pluralism in cognitive architectures and highlighting implications for AI ethics.

title: Dynamic Fusion-Aware Graph Convolutional Neural Network for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations
summary: DF-GCN dynamically adjusts neural network parameters for different emotion types in multimodal conversations by integrating differential equations with graph neural networks. This approach overcomes the limitation of fixed fusion weights, enabling emotion-specific parameter adaptation and improving classification accuracy across diverse emotional expressions in conversations.
content:
arXiv:2603.22345v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations (MERC) aims to identify and understand the emotions expressed by speakers during utterance interaction from multiple modalities (e.g., text, audio, images, etc.). Existing studies have shown that GCN can improve the performance of MERC by modeling dependencies between speakers. However, existing methods usually use fixed parameters to process multimodal features for different emotion types, ignoring the dynamics of fusion between different modalities, which forces the model to balance performance between multiple emotion categories, thus limiting the model's performance on some specific emotions. To this end, we propose a dynamic fusion-aware graph convolutional neural network (DF-GCN) for robust recognition of multimodal emotion features in conversations. Specifically, DF-GCN integrates ordinary differential equations into graph convolutional networks (GCNs) to {capture} the dynamic nature of emotional dependencies within utterance interaction networks and leverages the prompts generated by the global information vector (GIV) of the utterance to guide the dynamic fusion of multimodal features. This allows our model to dynamically change parameters when processing each utterance feature, so that different network parameters can be equipped for different emotion categories in the inference stage, thereby achieving more flexible emotion classification and enhancing the generalization ability of the model. Comprehensive experiments conducted on two public multimodal conversational datasets {confirm} that the proposed DF-GCN model delivers superior performance, benefiting significantly from the dynamic fusion mechanism introduced.

zh.title: 用于对话中多模态情感识别的动态融合感知图卷积神经网络
zh.summary: DF-GCN通过将微分方程与图神经网络相集成，动态地调整多模态对话中不同情感类型的神经网络参数。这种方法克服了固定融合权重的局限性，实现了特定情感的参数适配，提高了对话中不同情感表达的分类准确性。

fr.title: Réseau de Convolution Graphique Conscient de la Fusion Dynamique pour la Reconnaissance des Émotions Multimodales dans les Conversations
fr.summary: DF-GCN ajuste dynamiquement les paramètres du réseau de neurones pour différents types d'émotion dans les conversations multimodales en intégrant les équations différentielles avec les réseaux de neurones graphiques. Cette approche surmonte la limitation des poids de fusion fixes, permettant une adaptation des paramètres spécifique aux émotions et améliorant la précision de la classification dans les expressions émotionnelles diverses dans les conversations.

de.title: Dynamic Fusion-Aware Graph Convolutional Neural Network für multimodale Emotionserkennung in Gesprächen
de.summary: DF-GCN passt dynamisch Neuronennetzwerk-Parameter für verschiedene Emotionstypen in multimodalen Gesprächen an, indem Differentialgleichungen mit Graphenneuronennetzwerken integriert werden. Dieser Ansatz überwindet die Einschränkung fester Fusionsgewichte, ermöglicht emotions-spezifische Parameteranpassung und verbessert die Klassifizierungsgenauigkeit über diverse emotionale Ausdrücke in Gesprächen.

es.title: Red Neuronal Convolucional Gráfica Consciente de Fusión Dinámica para el Reconocimiento de Emociones Multimodales en Conversaciones
es.summary: DF-GCN ajusta dinámicamente los parámetros de la red neuronal para diferentes tipos de emociones en conversaciones multimodales integrando ecuaciones diferenciales con redes neuronales gráficas. Este enfoque supera la limitación de pesos de fusión fijos, permitiendo una adaptación de parámetros específica para las emociones y mejorando la precisión de clasificación en diversas expresiones emocionales en conversaciones.

title: Intelligence Inertia: Physical Principles and Applications
summary: The paper introduces intelligence inertia as a fundamental principle explaining why maintaining symbolic interpretability during system reconfiguration incurs super-linear computational costs, modeled as a relativistic "computational wall." The framework provides a unified physical explanation for adaptation overhead in intelligent systems, validated through experiments on neural architecture evolution and training optimization.
content:
arXiv:2603.22347v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While Landauer's principle establishes the fundamental thermodynamic floor for information erasure and Fisher Information provides a metric for local curvature in parameter space, these classical frameworks function effectively only as approximations within regimes of sparse rule-constraints. They fail to explain the super-linear, and often explosive, computational and energy costs incurred when maintaining symbolic interpretability during the reconfiguration of advanced intelligent systems. This paper introduces the property of intelligence inertia and its underlying physical principles as foundational characteristics for quantifying the computational weight of intelligence. We demonstrate that this phenomenon is not merely an empirical observation but originates from the fundamental non-commutativity between rules and states, a root cause we have formally organized into a rigorous mathematical framework. By analyzing the growing discrepancy between actual adaptation costs and static information-theoretic estimates, we derive a non-linear cost formula that mirrors the Lorentz factor, characterizing a relativistic J-shaped inflation curve -- a "computational wall" that static models are blind to. The validity of these physical principles is examined through a trilogy of decisive experiments: (1) a comparative adjudication of this J-curve inflation against classical Fisher Information models, (2) a geometric analysis of the "Zig-Zag" trajectory of neural architecture evolution, and (3) the implementation of an inertia-aware scheduler wrapper that optimizes the training of deep networks by respecting the agent's physical resistance to change. Our results suggest a unified physical description for the cost of structural adaptation, offering a first-principle explanation for the computational and interpretability-maintenance overhead in intelligent agents.

zh.title: 智能惯性：物理原理与应用
zh.summary: 该论文引入智能惯性作为基本原理，解释了在系统重新配置过程中维护符号可解释性为何会产生超线性计算成本，建模为相对论性的"计算壁垒"。该框架为智能系统的适应开销提供了统一的物理解释，通过神经架构演化和训练优化的实验验证了该框架。

fr.title: Inertie de l'intelligence : Principes physiques et applications
fr.summary: L'article introduit l'inertie de l'intelligence comme principe fondamental expliquant pourquoi le maintien de l'interprétabilité symbolique lors de la reconfiguration du système entraîne des coûts informatiques super-linéaires, modélisés comme un "mur informatique" relativiste. Le cadre fournit une explication physique unifiée des frais d'adaptation dans les systèmes intelligents, validée par des expériences sur l'évolution de l'architecture neuronale et l'optimisation de l'entraînement.

de.title: Intelligenzträgheit: Physikalische Prinzipien und Anwendungen
de.summary: Das Papier führt Intelligenzträgheit als Grundprinzip ein, das erklärt, warum die Aufrechterhaltung der symbolischen Interpretierbarkeit während der Neukonfiguration des Systems überlineare Rechenkosten verursacht, modelliert als relativistische "Rechenwand". Das Framework bietet eine einheitliche physikalische Erklärung für Anpassungsgemeinkosten in intelligenten Systemen, validiert durch Experimente zur neuronalen Architekturentwicklung und Trainingsoptimierung.

es.title: Inercia de la Inteligencia: Principios Físicos y Aplicaciones
es.summary: El artículo introduce la inercia de la inteligencia como un principio fundamental que explica por qué mantener la interpretabilidad simbólica durante la reconfiguración del sistema incurre en costos computacionales superlineales, modelados como un "muro computacional" relativista. El marco proporciona una explicación física unificada para la sobrecarga de adaptación en sistemas inteligentes, validada mediante experimentos sobre evolución de arquitectura neuronal y optimización del entrenamiento.

title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Temporal Authorization for Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates
summary: Session Risk Memory (SRM) extends stateless safety gates for AI agents by tracking behavioral trajectories to detect distributed attacks that decompose harmful intent across multiple individually-compliant steps. Operating with negligible overhead, SRM achieves perfect precision and recall in detecting exfiltration, privilege escalation, and compliance drift, introducing session-level authorization beyond per-action checks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22350v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deterministic pre-execution safety gates evaluate whether individual agent actions are compatible with their assigned roles. While effective at per-action authorization, these systems are structurally blind to distributed attacks that decompose harmful intent across multiple individually-compliant steps. This paper introduces Session Risk Memory (SRM), a lightweight deterministic module that extends stateless execution gates with trajectory-level authorization. SRM maintains a compact semantic centroid representing the evolving behavioral profile of an agent session and accumulates a risk signal through exponential moving average over baseline-subtracted gate outputs. It operates on the same semantic vector representation as the underlying gate, requiring no additional model components, training, or probabilistic inference. We evaluate SRM on a multi-turn benchmark of 80 sessions containing slow-burn exfiltration, gradual privilege escalation, and compliance drift scenarios. Results show that ILION+SRM achieves F1 = 1.0000 with 0% false positive rate, compared to stateless ILION at F1 = 0.9756 with 5% FPR, while maintaining 100% detection rate for both systems. Critically, SRM eliminates all false positives with a per-turn overhead under 250 microseconds. The framework introduces a conceptual distinction between spatial authorization consistency (evaluated per action) and temporal authorization consistency (evaluated over trajectory), providing a principled basis for session-level safety in agentic systems.

zh.title: 会话风险记忆（SRM）：确定性前执行安全门的时间授权
zh.summary: 会话风险记忆（SRM）通过追踪行为轨迹来扩展AI代理的无状态安全门，以检测将有害意图分解到多个单独合规步骤中的分布式攻击。SRM以可忽略的开销运行，在检测数据泄露、权限升级和合规漂移方面达到完美的精度和召回率，引入了超越逐个行动检查的会话级授权。

fr.title: Mémoire des risques de session (SRM) : Autorisation temporelle pour les portes de sécurité pré-exécution déterministes
fr.summary: La mémoire des risques de session (SRM) étend les portes de sécurité sans état pour les agents d'IA en suivi des trajectoires comportementales pour détecter les attaques distribuées qui décomposent l'intention malveillante sur plusieurs étapes individuellement conformes. Fonctionnant avec un surcoût négligeable, SRM atteint une précision et un rappel parfaits dans la détection de l'exfiltration, de l'escalade des privilèges et de la dérive de conformité, introduisant l'autorisation au niveau de la session au-delà des contrôles par action.

de.title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Zeitliche Autorisierung für deterministische Pre-Execution-Sicherheitstore
de.summary: Session Risk Memory (SRM) erweitert zustandslose Sicherheitstore für KI-Agenten durch Verfolgung von Verhaltenstrajektorien, um verteilte Angriffe zu erkennen, die schädliche Absicht auf mehrere individuell konforme Schritte verteilen. Mit vernachlässigbarem Overhead erreicht SRM perfekte Genauigkeit und Rückruf beim Erkennen von Datenabfluss, Privilegienerweiterung und Compliance-Versatz und führt Autorisierung auf Sitzungsebene über Kontrollen pro Aktion hinaus ein.

es.title: Memoria de Riesgo de Sesión (SRM): Autorización Temporal para Puertas de Seguridad Pre-Ejecución Deterministas
es.summary: La Memoria de Riesgo de Sesión (SRM) extiende las puertas de seguridad sin estado para agentes de IA rastreando trayectorias de comportamiento para detectar ataques distribuidos que descomponen la intención dañina en múltiples pasos individualmente cumplidos. Operando con una sobrecarga insignificante, SRM logra una precisión y recuperación perfectas al detectar exfiltración, escalada de privilegios y deriva de cumplimiento, introduciendo autorización a nivel de sesión más allá de controles por acción.

title: STEM Agent: A Self-Adapting, Tool-Enabled, Extensible Architecture for Multi-Protocol AI Agent Systems
summary: STEM Agent is a modular AI framework inspired by biological cell differentiation that unifies five interaction protocols, learns user behavioral preferences, and implements a skills acquisition system where recurring patterns crystallize into reusable capabilities. The architecture externalizes domain capabilities through standardized protocols and includes memory consolidation designed for sub-linear growth over extended interactions.
content:
arXiv:2603.22359v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Current AI agent frameworks commit early to a single interaction protocol, a fixed tool integration strategy, and static user models, limiting their deployment across diverse interaction paradigms. To address these constraints, we introduce STEM Agent (Self-adapting, Tool-enabled, Extensible, Multi-agent), a modular architecture inspired by biological pluripotency in which an undifferentiated agent core differentiates into specialized protocol handlers, tool bindings, and memory subsystems that compose into a fully functioning AI system. The framework unifies five interoperability protocols (A2A, AG-UI, A2UI, UCP, and AP2) behind a single gateway, introduces a Caller Profiler that continuously learns user preferences across more than twenty behavioral dimensions, externalizes all domain capabilities through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and implements a biologically inspired skills acquisition system in which recurring interaction patterns crystallize into reusable agent skills through a maturation lifecycle analogous to cell differentiation. Complementing these capabilities, the memory system incorporates consolidation mechanisms, including episodic pruning, semantic deduplication, and pattern extraction, designed for sub-linear growth under sustained interaction. A comprehensive 413-test suite validates protocol handler behavior and component integration across all five architectural layers, completing in under three seconds.

zh.title: STEM代理：一个自适应、工具启用、可扩展的多协议AI代理系统架构
zh.summary: STEM代理是一个受生物细胞分化启发的模块化AI框架，统一了五种交互协议，学习用户行为偏好，并实现了一个技能获取系统，其中重复的模式结晶成可重用的能力。该架构通过标准化协议外部化域能力，并包括为长期交互中的次线性增长而设计的内存整合。

fr.title: STEM Agent : Une Architecture Modulaire, Activée par des Outils, Extensible pour les Systèmes d'Agents IA Multi-Protocoles
fr.summary: STEM Agent est un framework d'IA modulaire inspiré par la différenciation cellulaire biologique qui unifie cinq protocoles d'interaction, apprend les préférences de comportement des utilisateurs et implémente un système d'acquisition de compétences où les schémas récurrents se cristallisent en capacités réutilisables. L'architecture externalise les capacités du domaine par des protocoles standardisés et inclut une consolidation de la mémoire conçue pour une croissance sous-linéaire sur les interactions étendues.

de.title: STEM Agent: Eine selbstanpassende, werkzeuggestützte, erweiterbare Architektur für Multi-Protokoll-KI-Agentensysteme
de.summary: STEM Agent ist ein modulares KI-Framework, inspiriert durch biologische Zelldifferenzierung, das fünf Interaktionsprotokolle vereinigt, Benutzerverhaltenspräferenzen erlernt und ein Fähigkeitserwerbbssystem implementiert, bei dem wiederkehrende Muster sich zu wiederverwendbaren Fähigkeiten kristallisieren. Die Architektur externalisiert Domänenfähigkeiten durch standardisierte Protokolle und enthält eine Speicherkonsolidierung, die für sublineares Wachstum über erweiterte Interaktionen hinweg ausgelegt ist.

es.title: STEM Agent: Una Arquitectura Autoadaptativa, Habilitada por Herramientas, Extensible para Sistemas Multiprotocolo de Agentes de IA
es.summary: STEM Agent es un framework de IA modular inspirado en la diferenciación celular biológica que unifica cinco protocolos de interacción, aprende las preferencias de comportamiento del usuario e implementa un sistema de adquisición de habilidades donde los patrones recurrentes se cristalizan en capacidades reutilizables. La arquitectura externaliza las capacidades del dominio a través de protocolos estandarizados e incluye consolidación de memoria diseñada para el crecimiento sublineal en interacciones extendidas.

title: From Static Templates to Dynamic Runtime Graphs: A Survey of Workflow Optimization for LLM Agents
summary: This survey reviews methods for designing and optimizing LLM-based workflows, distinguishing static reusable templates from dynamic approaches that adapt during execution. It provides unified vocabulary for positioning optimization methods and proposes structure-aware evaluation metrics that complement traditional task performance metrics with graph-level properties and robustness measures.
content:
arXiv:2603.22386v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are becoming increasingly popular for solving tasks by constructing executable workflows that interleave LLM calls, information retrieval, tool use, code execution, memory updates, and verification. This survey reviews recent methods for designing and optimizing such workflows, which we treat as agentic computation graphs (ACGs). We organize the literature based on when workflow structure is determined, where structure refers to which components or agents are present, how they depend on each other, and how information flows between them. This lens distinguishes static methods, which fix a reusable workflow scaffold before deployment, from dynamic methods, which select, generate, or revise the workflow for a particular run before or during execution. We further organize prior work along three dimensions: when structure is determined, what part of the workflow is optimized, and which evaluation signals guide optimization (e.g., task metrics, verifier signals, preferences, or trace-derived feedback). We also distinguish reusable workflow templates, run-specific realized graphs, and execution traces, separating reusable design choices from the structures actually deployed in a given run and from realized runtime behavior. Finally, we outline a structure-aware evaluation perspective that complements downstream task metrics with graph-level properties, execution cost, robustness, and structural variation across inputs. Our goal is to provide a clear vocabulary, a unified framework for positioning new methods, a more comparable view of existing body of literature, and a more reproducible evaluation standard for future work in workflow optimizations for LLM agents.

zh.title: 从静态模板到动态运行时图：LLM代理工作流优化综述
zh.summary: 本综述回顾了设计和优化基于LLM的工作流的方法，区分了静态可重用模板和在执行过程中适应的动态方法。它为定位优化方法提供了统一的词汇表，并提出了结构感知的评估指标，用图级属性和鲁棒性度量补充了传统的任务性能指标。

fr.title: Des Modèles Statiques aux Graphes Dynamiques à l'Exécution : Une Enquête sur l'Optimisation des Flux de Travail pour les Agents LLM
fr.summary: Cet article examine les méthodes de conception et d'optimisation des flux de travail basés sur les LLM, en distinguant les modèles réutilisables statiques des approches dynamiques qui s'adaptent pendant l'exécution. Il fournit un vocabulaire unifié pour positionner les méthodes d'optimisation et propose des métriques d'évaluation conscientes de la structure qui complètent les métriques traditionnelles de performance des tâches par des propriétés au niveau du graphe et des mesures de robustesse.

de.title: Von statischen Vorlagen zu dynamischen Laufzeitgraphen: Eine Übersicht der Workflow-Optimierung für LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Diese Übersicht behandelt Methoden zur Gestaltung und Optimierung LLM-basierter Workflows und unterscheidet zwischen statischen wiederverwendbaren Vorlagen und dynamischen Ansätzen, die sich während der Ausführung anpassen. Sie bietet ein einheitliches Vokabular zur Positionierung von Optimierungsmethoden und schlägt strukturbewusste Bewertungsmetriken vor, die traditionelle Aufgabenleistungsmetriken durch Eigenschaften auf Graphebene und Robustheitsmessungen ergänzen.

es.title: De Plantillas Estáticas a Grafos Dinámicos en Tiempo de Ejecución: Una Encuesta sobre Optimización de Flujos de Trabajo para Agentes LLM
es.summary: Esta encuesta revisa métodos para diseñar y optimizar flujos de trabajo basados en LLM, distinguiendo plantillas reutilizables estáticas de enfoques dinámicos que se adaptan durante la ejecución. Proporciona un vocabulario unificado para posicionar métodos de optimización y propone métricas de evaluación conscientes de la estructura que complementan métricas tradicionales de rendimiento de tareas con propiedades a nivel de gráfico y medidas de robustez.

title: Computational Arbitrage in AI Model Markets
summary: The paper demonstrates that arbitrageurs can profitably undercut AI model provider markets by efficiently allocating inference budget across competing providers, achieving profit margins up to 40% in verifiable domains. These strategies reduce market segmentation while enabling smaller providers to capture revenue earlier and driving down consumer prices.
content:
arXiv:2603.22404v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Consider a market of competing model providers selling query access to models with varying costs and capabilities. Customers submit problem instances and are willing to pay up to a budget for a verifiable solution. An arbitrageur efficiently allocates inference budget across providers to undercut the market, thus creating a competitive offering with no model-development risk. In this work, we initiate the study of arbitrage in AI model markets, empirically demonstrating the viability of arbitrage and illustrating its economic consequences. We conduct an in-depth case study of SWE-bench GitHub issue resolution using two representative models, GPT-5 mini and DeepSeek v3.2. In this verifiable domain, simple arbitrage strategies generate net profit margins of up to 40%. Robust arbitrage strategies that generalize across different domains remain profitable. Distillation further creates strong arbitrage opportunities, potentially at the expense of the teacher model's revenue. Multiple competing arbitrageurs drive down consumer prices, reducing the marginal revenue of model providers. At the same time, arbitrage reduces market segmentation and facilitates market entry for smaller model providers by enabling earlier revenue capture. Our results suggest that arbitrage can be a powerful force in AI model markets with implications for model development, distillation, and deployment.

zh.title: AI模型市场中的计算套利
zh.summary: 该论文展示了套利者可以通过在竞争提供商之间有效分配推理预算来盈利地制造AI模型提供商市场，在可验证的领域实现高达40%的利润率。这些策略减少了市场分割，同时使较小的提供商能够更早地获取收入，并推动降低消费者价格。

fr.title: Arbitrage Informatique sur les Marchés de Modèles IA
fr.summary: L'article démontre que les arbitragistes peuvent réduire les prix sur les marchés des fournisseurs de modèles IA en allouant efficacement le budget d'inférence entre les fournisseurs concurrents, réalisant des marges bénéficiaires jusqu'à 40 % dans les domaines vérifiables. Ces stratégies réduisent la segmentation du marché tout en permettant aux petits fournisseurs de capturer les revenus plus tôt et de réduire les prix à la consommation.

de.title: Rechnerisches Arbitrage auf KI-Modellmärkten
de.summary: Das Papier zeigt, dass Arbitrageure AI-Modell-Anbietermärkte profitabel unterbieten können, indem sie das Inferenzbudget effizient zwischen konkurrierenden Anbietern verteilen und Gewinnmargen von bis zu 40% in überprüfbaren Bereichen erzielen. Diese Strategien reduzieren die Marktsegmentierung und ermöglichen es kleineren Anbietern, Einnahmen früher zu erfassen und Verbraucherpreise zu senken.

es.title: Arbitraje Computacional en Mercados de Modelos de IA
es.summary: El artículo demuestra que los arbitrajistas pueden socavar de manera rentable los mercados de proveedores de modelos de IA asignando eficientemente el presupuesto de inferencia entre proveedores competidores, logrando márgenes de ganancia de hasta el 40% en dominios verificables. Estas estrategias reducen la segmentación del mercado mientras permiten que proveedores más pequeños capten ingresos antes e impulsen la reducción de precios para los consumidores.

title: Maximum Entropy Relaxation of Multi-Way Cardinality Constraints for Synthetic Population Generation
summary: This work proposes a maximum-entropy relaxation approach for generating synthetic populations that satisfy multiple overlapping constraints from surveys and data sources, reformulating the problem as convex optimization. The method outperforms classical approaches as constraint complexity increases, with applications to microsimulation, policy analysis, and privacy-preserving data release.
content:
arXiv:2603.22558v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generating synthetic populations from aggregate statistics is a core component of microsimulation, agent-based modeling, policy analysis, and privacy-preserving data release. Beyond classical census marginals, many applications require matching heterogeneous unary, binary, and ternary constraints derived from surveys, expert knowledge, or automatically extracted descriptions. Constructing populations that satisfy such multi-way constraints simultaneously poses a significant computational challenge. We consider populations where each individual is described by categorical attributes and the target is a collection of global frequency constraints over attribute combinations. Exact formulations scale poorly as the number and arity of constraints increase, especially when the constraints are numerous and overlapping. Grounded in methods from statistical physics, we propose a maximum-entropy relaxation of this problem. Multi-way cardinality constraints are matched in expectation rather than exactly, yielding an exponential-family distribution over complete population assignments and a convex optimization problem over Lagrange multipliers. We evaluate the approach on NPORS-derived scaling benchmarks with 4 to 40 attributes and compare it primarily against generalized raking. The results show that MaxEnt becomes increasingly advantageous as the number of attributes and ternary interactions grows, while raking remains competitive on smaller, lower-arity instances.

zh.title: 用于合成人口生成的多路基数约束的最大熵松弛
zh.summary: 本工作提出了一种最大熵松弛方法，用于生成满足来自调查和数据源的多个重叠约束的合成人口，将问题重新表述为凸优化问题。随着约束复杂性的增加，该方法优于经典方法，在微观模拟、政策分析和隐私保护数据发布中具有应用。

fr.title: Relaxation d'Entropie Maximale des Contraintes de Cardinalité Multidirectionnelles pour la Génération de Populations Synthétiques
fr.summary: Ce travail propose une approche de relaxation d'entropie maximale pour générer des populations synthétiques satisfaisant de multiples contraintes chevauchantes provenant d'enquêtes et de sources de données, reformulant le problème comme une optimisation convexe. La méthode surpasse les approches classiques à mesure que la complexité des contraintes augmente, avec des applications à la microsimulation, l'analyse des politiques et la publication de données préservant la vie privée.

de.title: Maximale-Entropie-Relaxation von Multi-Way-Kardinalitätsbeschränkungen für die Erzeugung von Synthesepopulationen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit schlägt einen Ansatz der Maximale-Entropie-Relaxation zur Erzeugung synthetischer Populationen vor, die mehrere überlappende Einschränkungen aus Umfragen und Datenquellen erfüllen, und formuliert das Problem als konvexe Optimierung um. Die Methode übertrifft klassische Ansätze mit zunehmender Komplexität der Einschränkungen und hat Anwendungen in der Mikrosimulation, Politikanalyse und datenschutzgerechter Datenveröffentlichung.

es.title: Relajación de Entropía Máxima de Restricciones de Cardinalidad Multidireccional para Generación de Población Sintética
es.summary: Este trabajo propone un enfoque de relajación de entropía máxima para generar poblaciones sintéticas que satisfacen múltiples restricciones superpuestas de encuestas y fuentes de datos, reformulando el problema como optimización convexa. El método supera a los enfoques clásicos a medida que aumenta la complejidad de las restricciones, con aplicaciones a microsimulación, análisis de políticas y divulgación de datos que preserva la privacidad.

title: AI Mental Models: Learned Intuition and Deliberation in a Bounded Neural Architecture
summary: The paper demonstrates that a bounded neural architecture can develop separate intuition and deliberation pathways for syllogistic reasoning, with deliberation achieving superior performance through sparse, structured internal representations. The findings suggest neural networks develop reasoning-like organization under architectural constraints, advancing understanding of multi-stage reasoning in AI systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.22561v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper asks whether a bounded neural architecture can exhibit a meaningful division of labor between intuition and deliberation on a classic 64-item syllogistic reasoning benchmark. More broadly, the benchmark is relevant to ongoing debates about world models and multi-stage reasoning in AI. It provides a controlled setting for testing whether a learned system can develop structured internal computation rather than only one-shot associative prediction. Experiment 1 evaluates a direct neural baseline for predicting full 9-way human response distributions under 5-fold cross-validation. Experiment 2 introduces a bounded dual-path architecture with separate intuition and deliberation pathways, motivated by computational mental-model theory (Khemlani & Johnson-Laird, 2022). Under cross-validation, bounded intuition reaches an aggregate correlation of r = 0.7272, whereas bounded deliberation reaches r = 0.8152, and the deliberation advantage is significant across folds (p = 0.0101). The largest held-out gains occur for NVC, Eca, and Oca, suggesting improved handling of rejection responses and c-a conclusions. A canonical 80:20 interpretability run and a five-seed stability sweep further indicate that the deliberation pathway develops sparse, differentiated internal structure, including an Oac-leaning state, a dominant workhorse state, and several weakly used or unused states whose exact indices vary across runs. These findings are consistent with reasoning-like internal organization under bounded conditions, while stopping short of any claim that the model reproduces full sequential processes of model construction, counterexample search, and conclusion revision.

zh.title: AI心智模型：有界神经结构中的学习直觉和审议推理
zh.summary: 论文表明有界神经结构可以为三段论推理开发分离的直觉和审议途径，审议通过稀疏、结构化的内部表征实现更优性能。研究结果表明神经网络在架构约束下会发展出推理样的组织方式，推进了对AI系统多阶段推理的理解。

fr.title: Modèles mentaux d'IA : Intuition apprise et délibération dans une architecture neurale bornée
fr.summary: L'article démontre qu'une architecture neurale bornée peut développer des voies d'intuition et de délibération séparées pour le raisonnement syllogistique, la délibération réalisant des performances supérieures grâce à des représentations internes clairsemées et structurées. Les résultats suggèrent que les réseaux de neurones développent une organisation de type raisonnement sous contraintes architecturales, ce qui fait progresser la compréhension du raisonnement multi-étapes dans les systèmes d'IA.

de.title: KI-Mentalmodelle: Erlernte Intuition und Überlegung in einer beschränkten neuronalen Architektur
de.summary: Das Papier zeigt, dass eine beschränkte neuronale Architektur separate Intuitions- und Überlegungspfade für syllogistisches Schlussfolgern entwickeln kann, wobei Überlegung durch spärliche, strukturierte interne Darstellungen eine überlegene Leistung erreicht. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass neuronale Netze unter architektonischen Beschränkungen eine schlussfolgerungsähnliche Organisation entwickeln und das Verständnis des mehrstufigen Schlussfolgerns in KI-Systemen vorantreiben.

es.title: Modelos mentales de IA: Intuición aprendida y deliberación en una arquitectura neuronal acotada
es.summary: El documento demuestra que una arquitectura neuronal acotada puede desarrollar vías separadas de intuición y deliberación para el razonamiento silogístico, logrando la deliberación un desempeño superior mediante representaciones internas escasas y estructuradas. Los hallazgos sugieren que las redes neuronales desarrollan una organización tipo razonamiento bajo restricciones arquitectónicas, avanzando en la comprensión del razonamiento multietapa en sistemas de IA.

title: Understanding LLM Performance Degradation in Multi-Instance Processing: The Roles of Instance Count and Context Length
summary: This evaluation reveals LLMs experience gradual performance degradation with 20-100 instances and collapse at larger counts, with instance count having stronger impact than context length. The findings demonstrate that optimizing LLM performance for multi-instance processing requires addressing both factors, with particular emphasis on instance count limitations.
content:
arXiv:2603.22608v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Users often rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) for processing multiple documents or performing analysis over a number of instances. For example, analysing the overall sentiment of a number of movie reviews requires an LLM to process the sentiment of each review individually in order to provide a final aggregated answer. While LLM performance on such individual tasks is generally high, there has been little research on how LLMs perform when dealing with multi-instance inputs. In this paper, we perform a comprehensive evaluation of the multi-instance processing (MIP) ability of LLMs for tasks in which they excel individually. The results show that all LLMs follow a pattern of slight performance degradation for small numbers of instances (approximately 20-100), followed by a performance collapse on larger instance counts. Crucially, our analysis shows that while context length is associated with this degradation, the number of instances has a stronger effect on the final results. This finding suggests that when optimising LLM performance for MIP, attention should be paid to both context length and, in particular, instance count.

zh.title: 理解多实例处理中的大语言模型性能下降
zh.summary: 这项评估揭示了大语言模型在20-100个实例时会经历渐进性能下降，在更大数量时性能崩溃，实例数量的影响大于上下文长度。研究结果表明，优化大语言模型在多实例处理中的性能需要同时解决这两个因素，特别是实例数量的限制。

fr.title: Comprendre la dégradation des performances des LLM dans le traitement multi-instances
fr.summary: Cette évaluation révèle que les LLM connaissent une dégradation graduelle des performances avec 20-100 instances et s'effondrent à des nombres plus importants, le nombre d'instances ayant un impact plus fort que la longueur du contexte. Les résultats démontrent que l'optimisation des performances des LLM pour le traitement multi-instances nécessite d'aborder les deux facteurs, en mettant l'accent particulièrement sur les limitations du nombre d'instances.

de.title: Verständnis der Leistungsbeeinträchtigung von LLMs bei der Mehrinstanz-Verarbeitung
de.summary: Diese Evaluierung offenbart, dass LLMs bei 20-100 Instanzen eine allmähliche Leistungsbeeinträchtigung erfahren und bei größeren Anzahlen zusammenbrechen, wobei die Instanzanzahl einen stärkeren Einfluss hat als die Kontextlänge. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass die Optimierung der LLM-Leistung für die Mehrinstanz-Verarbeitung die Behandlung beider Faktoren erfordert, mit besonderem Schwerpunkt auf die Begrenzungen der Instanzanzahl.

es.title: Comprender la degradación del desempeño de LLM en el procesamiento multi-instancia
es.summary: Esta evaluación revela que los LLM experimentan una degradación gradual del desempeño con 20-100 instancias y colapso en números mayores, teniendo el número de instancias un impacto más fuerte que la longitud del contexto. Los hallazgos demuestran que optimizar el desempeño de LLM para el procesamiento multi-instancia requiere abordar ambos factores, con énfasis particular en las limitaciones del número de instancias.

title: Bridging the Know-Act Gap via Task-Level Autoregressive Reasoning
summary: The paper identifies a fundamental gap between LLMs' ability to discriminatively recognize flawed inputs and their failure to reflect this in generative responses, caused by token-level autoregression conflating task selection with content generation. DeIllusionLLM addresses this through task-level reasoning and self-distillation, reducing error-prone answers while maintaining general reasoning performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.22619v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLMs often generate seemingly valid answers to flawed or ill-posed inputs. This is not due to missing knowledge: under discriminative prompting, the same models can mostly identify such issues, yet fail to reflect this in standard generative responses. This reveals a fundamental know-act gap between discriminative recognition and generative behavior. Prior work largely characterizes this issue in narrow settings, such as math word problems or question answering, with limited focus on how to integrate these two modes. In this work, we present a comprehensive analysis using FaultyScience, a newly constructed large-scale, cross-disciplinary benchmark of faulty scientific questions. We show that the gap is pervasive and stems from token-level autoregression, which entangles task selection (validate vs. answer) with content generation, preventing discriminative knowledge from being utilized. To address this, we propose DeIllusionLLM, a task-level autoregressive framework that explicitly models this decision. Through self-distillation, the model unifies discriminative judgment and generative reasoning within a single backbone. Empirically, DeIllusionLLM substantially reduces answer-despite-error failures under natural prompting while maintaining general reasoning performance, demonstrating that self-distillation is an effective and scalable solution for bridging the discriminative-generative know-act gap

zh.title: 通过任务级自回归推理弥合认知-行动差距
zh.summary: 论文识别出大语言模型在区别性识别有缺陷输入的能力与其在生成响应中反映这一点的失败之间的根本差距，由令牌级自回归导致的任务选择与内容生成的混淆。DeIllusionLLM通过任务级推理和自蒸馏解决此问题，减少容易出错的答案同时维持一般推理性能。

fr.title: Combler l'écart savoir-agir par le raisonnement autorégressif au niveau des tâches
fr.summary: L'article identifie un écart fondamental entre la capacité des LLM à reconnaître discriminativement les entrées défectueuses et leur échec à le refléter dans les réponses génératives, causé par l'autorégression au niveau des tokens confondant la sélection des tâches avec la génération de contenu. DeIllusionLLM résout ce problème grâce au raisonnement au niveau des tâches et à l'auto-distillation, réduisant les réponses sujettes aux erreurs tout en maintenant les performances de raisonnement générales.

de.title: Schließen der Wissens-Handlungs-Lücke durch aufgabenebene autoregressive Argumentation
de.summary: Das Papier identifiziert eine grundlegende Lücke zwischen der Fähigkeit von LLMs, fehlerhafte Eingaben diskriminativ zu erkennen, und ihrem Versagen, dies in generativen Antworten widerzuspiegeln, verursacht durch Token-Ebenen-Autoregression, die Aufgabenauswahl mit Content-Generierung vermischt. DeIllusionLLM behebt dies durch aufgabenebene Argumentation und Selbst-Destillation, reduziert fehleranfällige Antworten bei Beibehaltung der allgemeinen Argumentationsleistung.

es.title: Cerrando la brecha de saber-actuar mediante razonamiento autoregresivo a nivel de tareas
es.summary: El documento identifica una brecha fundamental entre la capacidad de los LLM de reconocer discriminativamente entradas defectuosas y su fracaso en reflejar esto en respuestas generativas, causada por la autoregresión a nivel de token que confunde la selección de tareas con la generación de contenido. DeIllusionLLM aborda esto mediante razonamiento a nivel de tareas y auto-destilación, reduciendo respuestas propensas a errores mientras se mantiene el desempeño de razonamiento general.

title: Graph-Aware Late Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Biomedical Literature
summary: GraLC-RAG improves biomedical RAG systems by incorporating structural awareness and knowledge graphs to retrieve evidence from multiple document sections, not just the single most relevant chunk. Experiments show structure-aware methods retrieve from 15.6x more sections while maintaining quality, exposing limitations in ranking-only metrics for scientific document retrieval.
content:
arXiv:2603.22633v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for biomedical literature are typically evaluated using ranking metrics like Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), which measure how well the system identifies the single most relevant chunk. We argue that for full-text scientific documents, this paradigm is incomplete: it rewards retrieval precision while ignoring retrieval breadth -- the ability to surface evidence from across a document's structural sections. We propose GraLC-RAG, a framework that unifies late chunking with graph-aware structural intelligence, introducing structure-aware chunk boundary detection, UMLS knowledge graph infusion, and graph-guided hybrid retrieval. We evaluate six strategies on 2,359 IMRaD-filtered PubMed Central articles using 2,033 cross-section questions and two metric families: standard ranking metrics (MRR, Recall@k) and structural coverage metrics (SecCov@k, CS Recall). Our results expose a sharp divergence: content-similarity methods achieve the highest MRR (0.517) but always retrieve from a single section, while structure-aware methods retrieve from up to 15.6x more sections. Generation experiments show that KG-infused retrieval narrows the answer-quality gap to delta-F1 = 0.009 while maintaining 4.6x section diversity. These findings demonstrate that standard metrics systematically undervalue structural retrieval and that closing the multi-section synthesis gap is a key open problem for biomedical RAG.

zh.title: 用于生物医学文献检索增强生成的图感知后期分块方法
zh.summary: GraLC-RAG通过纳入结构感知和知识图谱来改进生物医学RAG系统，从多个文档部分检索证据，而不仅仅是单一最相关的块。实验表明，结构感知方法检索自15.6倍以上的部分同时保持质量，暴露了科学文档检索中仅排名指标的局限性。

fr.title: Chunking tardif conscient des graphes pour la génération augmentée par récupération dans la littérature biomédicale
fr.summary: GraLC-RAG améliore les systèmes RAG biomédicaux en incorporant une conscience structurelle et des graphes de connaissances pour récupérer des preuves à partir de plusieurs sections de documents, pas seulement du chunk le plus pertinent. Les expériences montrent que les méthodes conscientes de la structure récupèrent 15,6 fois plus de sections tout en maintenant la qualité, exposant les limitations des métriques de classement uniquement pour la récupération de documents scientifiques.

de.title: Graphbewusstes spätes Chunking für Retrieval-Augmented Generation in biomedizinischer Literatur
de.summary: GraLC-RAG verbessert biomedizinische RAG-Systeme durch die Einbeziehung von Strukturbewusstsein und Wissensgraphen, um Belege aus mehreren Dokumentabschnitten abzurufen, nicht nur aus dem einzelnen relevantesten Chunk. Experimente zeigen, dass strukturbewusste Methoden 15,6-mal mehr Abschnitte abrufen und dabei die Qualität beibehalten, wobei sie Einschränkungen rein ranglistenbasierter Metriken für das Abrufen wissenschaftlicher Dokumente aufdecken.

es.title: Chunking tardío consciente de gráficos para generación aumentada por recuperación en literatura biomédica
es.summary: GraLC-RAG mejora los sistemas RAG biomédicos incorporando conciencia estructural y gráficos de conocimiento para recuperar evidencia de múltiples secciones de documentos, no solo del fragmento más relevante. Los experimentos muestran que los métodos conscientes de la estructura recuperan 15,6 veces más secciones mientras mantienen la calidad, exponiendo limitaciones en métricas basadas únicamente en clasificación para la recuperación de documentos científicos.

title: Benchmarking Multi-Agent LLM Architectures for Financial Document Processing: A Comparative Study of Orchestration Patterns, Cost-Accuracy Tradeoffs and Production Scaling Strategies
summary: This benchmark compares four multi-agent orchestration patterns for extracting structured information from financial documents, showing reflexive architectures achieve highest accuracy (F1 0.943) but at 2.3x cost, while hierarchical approaches optimize cost-accuracy tradeoff. Hybrid configurations recover 89% of reflexive gains at only 1.15x baseline cost, providing practical guidance for production deployments.
content:
arXiv:2603.22651v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The adoption of large language models (LLMs) for structured information extraction from financial documents has accelerated rapidly, yet production deployments face fundamental architectural decisions with limited empirical guidance. We present a systematic benchmark comparing four multi-agent orchestration architectures: sequential pipeline, parallel fan-out with merge, hierarchical supervisor-worker and reflexive self-correcting loop. These are evaluated across five frontier and open-weight LLMs on a corpus of 10,000 SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q and 8-K forms). Our evaluation spans 25 extraction field types covering governance structures, executive compensation and financial metrics, measured along five axes: field-level F1, document-level accuracy, end-to-end latency, cost per document and token efficiency. We find that reflexive architectures achieve the highest field-level F1 (0.943) but at 2.3x the cost of sequential baselines, while hierarchical architectures occupy the most favorable position on the cost-accuracy Pareto frontier (F1 0.921 at 1.4x cost). We further present ablation studies on semantic caching, model routing and adaptive retry strategies, demonstrating that hybrid configurations can recover 89\% of the reflexive architecture's accuracy gains at only 1.15x baseline cost. Our scaling analysis from 1K to 100K documents per day reveals non-obvious throughput-accuracy degradation curves that inform capacity planning. These findings provide actionable guidance for practitioners deploying multi-agent LLM systems in regulated financial environments.

zh.title: 多智能体LLM架构用于财务文档处理的基准测试：编排模式、成本-准确性权衡和生产扩展策略的比较研究
zh.summary: 该基准测试比较了四种用于从财务文档中提取结构化信息的多智能体编排模式，显示反思性架构实现最高准确率（F1 0.943），但成本是2.3倍，而分层方法优化成本-准确性权衡。混合配置以仅1.15倍基线成本恢复反思性收益的89%，为生产部署提供实用指导。

fr.title: Benchmarking des architectures multi-agents LLM pour le traitement de documents financiers : une étude comparative des modèles d'orchestration, des compromis coût-précision et des stratégies de mise à l'échelle en production
fr.summary: Ce benchmark compare quatre modèles d'orchestration multi-agents pour extraire des informations structurées à partir de documents financiers, montrant que les architectures réflexives atteignent la plus haute précision (F1 0,943) mais à un coût 2,3x, tandis que les approches hiérarchiques optimisent le compromis coût-précision. Les configurations hybrides récupèrent 89% des gains réflexifs à seulement 1,15x le coût de base, fournissant des conseils pratiques pour les déploiements en production.

de.title: Benchmarking von Multi-Agent-LLM-Architekturen für die Finanzdokumentenverarbeitung: eine vergleichende Studie von Orchestrierungsmustern, Kosten-Genauigkeits-Kompromissen und Produktionsskalierungsstrategien
de.summary: Dieser Benchmark vergleicht vier Multi-Agent-Orchestrierungsmuster zum Extrahieren strukturierter Informationen aus Finanzdokumenten und zeigt, dass reflexive Architekturen die höchste Genauigkeit erreichen (F1 0,943), aber zu 2,3-facher Kosten, während hierarchische Ansätze den Kosten-Genauigkeits-Kompromiss optimieren. Hybridkonfigurationen erhalten 89% der reflexiven Gewinne bei nur 1,15-facher Baselinekosten und bieten praktische Anleitung für Produktionsbereitstellungen.

es.title: Evaluación comparativa de arquitecturas LLM multiagente para procesamiento de documentos financieros: un estudio comparativo de patrones de orquestación, compensaciones coste-precisión y estrategias de escalado en producción
es.summary: Este benchmark compara cuatro patrones de orquestación multiagente para extraer información estructurada de documentos financieros, mostrando que las arquitecturas reflexivas logran la mayor precisión (F1 0,943) pero a un costo 2,3x, mientras que los enfoques jerárquicos optimizan el compromiso costo-precisión. Las configuraciones híbridas recuperan el 89% de las ganancias reflexivas a solo 1,15x el costo de línea base, proporcionando orientación práctica para implementaciones en producción.

title: MuQ-Eval: An Open-Source Per-Sample Quality Metric for AI Music Generation Evaluation
summary: MUQ-EVAL is an open-source per-sample quality metric for AI-generated music that achieves high human correlation (SRCC = 0.838) while running in real time on consumer hardware. Encoder choice dominates design decisions over architecture and training strategies, and the metric enables personalized evaluators from limited user annotations.
content:
arXiv:2603.22677v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Distributional metrics such as Fr\'echet Audio Distance cannot score individual music clips and correlate poorly with human judgments, while the only per-sample learned metric achieving high human correlation is closed-source. We introduce MUQ-EVAL, an open-source per-sample quality metric for AIgenerated music built by training lightweight prediction heads on frozen MuQ-310M features using MusicEval, a dataset of generated clips from 31 text-to-music systems with expert quality ratings. Our simplest model, frozen features with attention pooling and a two-layer MLP, achieves system-level SRCC = 0.957 and utterance-level SRCC = 0.838 with human mean opinion scores. A systematic ablation over training objectives and adaptation strategies shows that no addition meaningfully improves the frozen baseline, indicating that frozen MuQ representations already capture quality-relevant information. Encoder choice is the dominant design factor, outweighing all architectural and training decisions. LoRA-adapted models trained on as few as 150 clips already achieve usable correlation, enabling personalized quality evaluators from individual listener annotations. A controlled degradation analysis reveals selective sensitivity to signal-level artifacts but insensitivity to musical-structural distortions. Our metric, MUQ-EVAL, is fully open-source, outperforms existing open per-sample metrics, and runs in real time on a single consumer GPU. Code, model weights, and evaluation scripts are available at https://github.com/dgtql/MuQ-Eval.

zh.title: MuQ-Eval：用于人工智能音乐生成评估的开源单样本质量指标
zh.summary: MUQ-EVAL是一个用于人工智能生成音乐的开源单样本质量指标，在消费类硬件上实时运行时实现高人类相关性（SRCC = 0.838）。编码器选择在架构和训练策略上主导设计决策，该指标可以从有限的用户注释启用个性化评估器。

fr.title: MuQ-Eval : une métrique de qualité open-source par échantillon pour l'évaluation de la génération de musique par IA
fr.summary: MUQ-EVAL est une métrique de qualité open-source par échantillon pour la musique générée par l'IA qui atteint une corrélation humaine élevée (SRCC = 0,838) tout en s'exécutant en temps réel sur du matériel grand public. Le choix de l'encodeur domine les décisions de conception par rapport à l'architecture et aux stratégies d'entraînement, et la métrique permet des évaluateurs personnalisés à partir d'annotations utilisateur limitées.

de.title: MuQ-Eval: eine quelloffene Pro-Stichproben-Qualitätsmetrik zur Evaluierung der KI-Musikgenerierung
de.summary: MUQ-EVAL ist eine quelloffene Pro-Stichproben-Qualitätsmetrik für KI-generierte Musik, die eine hohe menschliche Korrelation (SRCC = 0,838) erreicht, während sie in Echtzeit auf Standard-Hardware läuft. Die Encoderauswahl dominiert Designentscheidungen gegenüber Architektur- und Trainingsstrategien, und die Metrik ermöglicht personalisierte Bewerter aus begrenzten Benutzeranmerkungen.

es.title: MuQ-Eval: una métrica de calidad de código abierto por muestra para la evaluación de generación de música con IA
es.summary: MUQ-EVAL es una métrica de calidad de código abierto por muestra para música generada por IA que logra una alta correlación humana (SRCC = 0,838) mientras se ejecuta en tiempo real en hardware de consumo. La elección del codificador domina las decisiones de diseño sobre arquitectura y estrategias de entrenamiento, y la métrica permite evaluadores personalizados a partir de anotaciones de usuario limitadas.

title: HyFI: Hyperbolic Feature Interpolation for Brain-Vision Alignment
summary: Researchers propose HyFI, a novel framework using hyperbolic geometry to better align brain signals with visual features, achieving state-of-the-art results with accuracy improvements up to 17.3% on brain-to-image retrieval tasks. The work advances understanding of how the human visual system processes information.
content:
arXiv:2603.22721v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent progress in artificial intelligence has encouraged numerous attempts to understand and decode human visual system from brain signals. These prior works typically align neural activity independently with semantic and perceptual features extracted from images using pre-trained vision models. However, they fail to account for two key challenges: (1) the modality gap arising from the natural difference in the information level of representation between brain signals and images, and (2) the fact that semantic and perceptual features are highly entangled within neural activity. To address these issues, we utilize hyperbolic space, which is well-suited for considering differences in the amount of information and has the geometric property that geodesics between two points naturally bend toward the origin, where the representational capacity is lower. Leveraging these properties, we propose a novel framework, Hyperbolic Feature Interpolation (HyFI), which interpolates between semantic and perceptual visual features along hyperbolic geodesics. This enables both the fusion and compression of perceptual and semantic information, effectively reflecting the limited expressiveness of brain signals and the entangled nature of these features. As a result, it facilitates better alignment between brain and visual features. We demonstrate that HyFI achieves state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot brain-to-image retrieval, outperforming prior methods with Top-1 accuracy improvements of up to +17.3% on THINGS-EEG and +9.1% on THINGS-MEG.

zh.title: HyFI：脑视觉对齐的双曲特征插值
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了HyFI，一种使用双曲几何来更好地对齐脑信号与视觉特征的新框架，在脑图像检索任务上取得了最先进的结果，精度提高了17.3%。这项工作推进了对人类视觉系统如何处理信息的理解。

fr.title: HyFI : Interpolation de caractéristiques hyperboliques pour l'alignement cerveau-vision
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent HyFI, un nouveau cadre utilisant la géométrie hyperbolique pour mieux aligner les signaux cérébraux aux caractéristiques visuelles, obtenant des résultats de pointe avec des améliorations de précision jusqu'à 17,3 % sur les tâches de récupération cerveau-image. Le travail fait progresser la compréhension de la façon dont le système visuel humain traite les informations.

de.title: HyFI: Hyperbolische Merkmalsinterpolation für Gehirn-Vision-Ausrichtung
de.summary: Forscher schlagen HyFI vor, ein neuartiges Framework, das hyperbolische Geometrie verwendet, um Gehirnsignale besser mit visuellen Merkmalen auszurichten und erzielt hochmoderne Ergebnisse mit Genauigkeitsverbesserungen von bis zu 17,3% bei Gehirn-zu-Bild-Abrufaufgaben. Die Arbeit trägt zum Verständnis bei, wie das menschliche visuelle System Informationen verarbeitet.

es.title: HyFI: Interpolación de características hiperbólicas para la alineación cerebro-visión
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen HyFI, un marco novedoso que utiliza geometría hiperbólica para alinear mejor las señales cerebrales con características visuales, logrando resultados de última generación con mejoras de precisión de hasta el 17,3% en tareas de recuperación cerebro-imagen. El trabajo avanza la comprensión de cómo el sistema visual humano procesa la información.

title: Beyond Binary Correctness: Scaling Evaluation of Long-Horizon Agents on Subjective Enterprise Tasks
summary: Researchers introduce LH-Bench, an evaluation framework that moves beyond binary correctness to assess AI agents on subjective enterprise tasks like design-to-code and content creation. Expert-authored evaluation rubrics prove more reliable than LLM-authored ones, establishing better standards for assessing real-world agent capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.22744v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models excel on objectively verifiable tasks such as math and programming, where evaluation reduces to unit tests or a single correct answer. In contrast, real-world enterprise work is often subjective and context-dependent: success hinges on organizational goals, user intent, and the quality of intermediate artifacts produced across long, multi-tool workflows.
  We introduce LH-Bench, a three-pillar evaluation design that moves beyond binary correctness to score autonomous, long-horizon execution on subjective enterprise tasks. The pillars are: (i) expert-grounded rubrics that give LLM judges the domain context needed to score subjective work, (ii) curated ground-truth artifacts that enable stepwise reward signals (e.g., chapter-level annotation for content tasks), and (iii) pairwise human preference evaluation for convergent validation. We show that domain-authored rubrics provide substantially more reliable evaluation signals than LLM-authored rubrics (kappa = 0.60 vs. 0.46), and that human preference judgments confirm the same top-tier separation (p < 0.05), evidence that expert-grounded evaluation can scale without sacrificing reliability. We release public datasets and report results on two environments: Figma-to-code (33 real .fig tasks against the Figma API via MCP) and Programmatic content (41 courses comprising 183 individually-evaluated chapters on a course platform serving 30+ daily users).

zh.title: 超越二元正确性：长视界代理在主观企业任务上的评估扩展
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了LH-Bench，一个超越二元正确性的评估框架，用于评估AI代理在主观企业任务（如设计转代码和内容创建）上的表现。专家编写的评估标准被证明比LLM编写的更可靠，为评估真实世界代理能力建立了更好的标准。

fr.title: Au-delà de la correction binaire : mise à l'échelle de l'évaluation des agents à long horizon sur des tâches d'entreprise subjectives
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent LH-Bench, un cadre d'évaluation qui va au-delà de la correction binaire pour évaluer les agents IA sur des tâches d'entreprise subjectives comme la conception-code et la création de contenu. Les rubriques d'évaluation rédigées par des experts s'avèrent plus fiables que celles rédigées par des LLM, établissant de meilleures normes pour évaluer les capacités des agents du monde réel.

de.title: Jenseits der binären Korrektheit: Skalierung der Bewertung von Long-Horizon-Agenten bei subjektiven Unternehmensaufgaben
de.summary: Forscher führen LH-Bench ein, ein Bewertungsrahmen, der über binäre Korrektheit hinausgeht, um KI-Agenten bei subjektiven Unternehmensaufgaben wie Design-to-Code und Content-Erstellung zu bewerten. Von Experten verfasste Bewertungsrichtlinien erweisen sich als zuverlässiger als von LLM verfasste, und etablieren bessere Standards zur Bewertung der Fähigkeiten von Agenten in der Praxis.

es.title: Más allá de la corrección binaria: escalando la evaluación de agentes de horizonte largo en tareas empresariales subjetivas
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen LH-Bench, un marco de evaluación que va más allá de la corrección binaria para evaluar agentes de IA en tareas empresariales subjetivas como diseño a código y creación de contenido. Las rúbricas de evaluación redactadas por expertos resultan ser más confiables que las redactadas por LLM, estableciendo mejores estándares para evaluar las capacidades de agentes del mundo real.

title: CLiGNet: Clinical Label-Interaction Graph Network for Medical Specialty Classification from Clinical Transcriptions
summary: CLiGNet combines BERT with graph neural networks to classify clinical transcriptions into medical specialties more accurately while achieving better calibration. The work identifies and corrects a major data leakage flaw in the widely-used MTSamples benchmark, improving both clinical AI systems and evaluation standards.
content:
arXiv:2603.22752v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated classification of clinical transcriptions into medical specialties is essential for routing, coding, and clinical decision support, yet prior work on the widely used MTSamples benchmark suffers from severe data leakage caused by applying SMOTE oversampling before train test splitting. We first document this methodological flaw and establish a leakage free benchmark across 40 medical specialties (4966 records), revealing that the true task difficulty is substantially higher than previously reported.
  We then introduce CLiGNet (Clinical Label Interaction Graph Network), a neural architecture that combines a Bio ClinicalBERT text encoder with a two layer Graph Convolutional Network operating on a specialty label graph constructed from semantic similarity and ICD 10 chapter priors. Per label attention gates fuse document and label graph representations, trained with focal binary cross entropy loss to handle extreme class imbalance (181 to 1 ratio).
  Across seven baselines ranging from TF IDF classifiers to Clinical Longformer, CLiGNet without calibration achieves the highest macro F1 of 0.279, with an ablation study confirming that the GCN label graph provides the single largest component gain (increase of 0.066 macro F1). Adding per label Platt scaling calibration yields an expected calibration error of 0.007, demonstrating a principled trade off between ranking performance and probability reliability.
  We provide comprehensive failure analysis covering pairwise specialty confusions, rare class behaviour, document length effects, and token level Integrated Gradients attribution, offering actionable insights for clinical NLP system deployment.

zh.title: CLiGNet：用于临床转录文本医学专科分类的临床标签交互图神经网络
zh.summary: CLiGNet将BERT与图神经网络相结合，以更准确地将临床转录文本分类为医学专科，同时实现更好的校准。该工作识别并纠正了广泛使用的MTSamples基准中的一个重大数据泄露缺陷，改进了临床AI系统和评估标准。

fr.title: CLiGNet : Réseau de graphique d'interaction d'étiquettes cliniques pour la classification des spécialités médicales à partir des transcriptions cliniques
fr.summary: CLiGNet combine BERT avec des réseaux de neurones graphiques pour classer les transcriptions cliniques en spécialités médicales de manière plus précise tout en achieving une meilleure calibration. Le travail identifie et corrige un défaut majeur de fuite de données dans l'indice de référence MTSamples largement utilisé, améliorant à la fois les systèmes d'IA clinique et les normes d'évaluation.

de.title: CLiGNet: Klinisches Label-Interaktions-Graphennetzwerk für die medizinische Fachrichtungsklassifizierung aus klinischen Transkripten
de.summary: CLiGNet kombiniert BERT mit Graphenneuronalen Netzen, um klinische Transkripte genauer in medizinische Fachrichtungen zu klassifizieren und gleichzeitig eine bessere Kalibrierung zu erreichen. Die Arbeit identifiziert und korrigiert einen großen Datenlecks-Fehler in der weit verbreiteten MTSamples-Benchmark und verbessert sowohl klinische KI-Systeme als auch Bewertungsstandards.

es.title: CLiGNet: Red de Gráfico de Interacción de Etiqueta Clínica para la Clasificación de Especialidades Médicas a partir de Transcripciones Clínicas
es.summary: CLiGNet combina BERT con redes neuronales gráficas para clasificar las transcripciones clínicas en especialidades médicas de manera más precisa mientras logra una mejor calibración. El trabajo identifica y corrige una falla importante de fuga de datos en el ampliamente utilizado índice de referencia MTSamples, mejorando tanto los sistemas de IA clínica como los estándares de evaluación.

title: Can LLM Agents Generate Real-World Evidence? Evaluating Observational Studies in Medical Databases
summary: Researchers introduce RWE-bench, a benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on real medical research tasks using MIMIC-IV database, revealing current agents achieve only 39.9% success at best. The low performance highlights significant gaps in AI capabilities for critical end-to-end evidence generation in medical research.
content:
arXiv:2603.22767v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Observational studies can yield clinically actionable evidence at scale, but executing them on real-world databases is open-ended and requires coherent decisions across cohort construction, analysis, and reporting. Prior evaluations of LLM agents emphasize isolated steps or single answers, missing the integrity and internal structure of the resulting evidence bundle. To address this gap, we introduce RWE-bench, a benchmark grounded in MIMIC-IV and derived from peer-reviewed observational studies. Each task provides the corresponding study protocol as the reference standard, requiring agents to execute experiments in a real database and iteratively generate tree-structured evidence bundles. We evaluate six LLMs (three open-source, three closed-source) under three agent scaffolds using both question-level correctness and end-to-end task metrics. Across 162 tasks, task success is low: the best agent reaches 39.9%, and the best open-source model reaches 30.4%. Agent scaffolds also matter substantially, causing over 30% variation in performance metrics. Furthermore, we implement an automated cohort evaluation method to rapidly localize errors and identify agent failure modes. Overall, the results highlight persistent limitations in agents' ability to produce end-to-end evidence bundles, and efficient validation remains an important direction for future work. Code and data are available at https://github.com/somewordstoolate/RWE-bench.

zh.title: LLM代理能生成真实世界证据吗？评估医学数据库中的观察性研究
zh.summary: 研究人员推出了RWE-bench，一个用于评估LLM代理在使用MIMIC-IV数据库的真实医学研究任务上的基准，揭示当前代理最多只能达到39.9%的成功率。较低的性能突显了AI在医学研究中关键的端到端证据生成能力的显著差距。

fr.title: Les agents LLM peuvent-ils générer des preuves du monde réel ? Évaluation des études observationnelles dans les bases de données médicales
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent RWE-bench, un indice de référence pour évaluer les agents LLM sur des tâches de recherche médicale réelles utilisant la base de données MIMIC-IV, révélant que les agents actuels ne réussissent que 39,9% au mieux. La faible performance met en évidence les lacunes significatives des capacités de l'IA pour la génération de preuves critiques de bout en bout dans la recherche médicale.

de.title: Können LLM-Agenten echte Evidenz generieren? Bewertung von Beobachtungsstudien in medizinischen Datenbanken
de.summary: Forscher führen RWE-bench ein, eine Benchmark zur Bewertung von LLM-Agenten bei realen medizinischen Forschungsaufgaben mit der MIMIC-IV-Datenbank, die zeigt, dass aktuelle Agenten bestenfalls nur eine 39,9%-Erfolgsquote erreichen. Die schlechte Leistung unterstreicht erhebliche Lücken in den KI-Fähigkeiten für die kritische End-to-End-Evidenzgenerierung in der medizinischen Forschung.

es.title: ¿Pueden los agentes LLM generar evidencia del mundo real? Evaluación de estudios observacionales en bases de datos médicas
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen RWE-bench, un índice de referencia para evaluar agentes LLM en tareas reales de investigación médica utilizando la base de datos MIMIC-IV, revelando que los agentes actuales logran solo un 39,9% de éxito en el mejor de los casos. El bajo rendimiento destaca brechas significativas en las capacidades de IA para la generación crítica de evidencia de extremo a extremo en la investigación médica.

title: AgriPestDatabase-v1.0: A Structured Insect Dataset for Training Agricultural Large Language Model
summary: AgriPestDatabase and fine-tuned 7B language models enable offline pest management decision support for farmers with limited connectivity. Mistral 7B achieves 88.9% accuracy on agricultural Q/A tasks, demonstrating practical deployment of AI tools for underserved rural farming communities.
content:
arXiv:2603.22777v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agricultural pest management increasingly relies on timely and accurate access to expert knowledge, yet high quality labeled data and continuous expert support remain limited, particularly for farmers operating in rural regions with unstable/no internet connectivity. At the same time, the rapid growth of AI and LLMs has created new opportunities to deliver practical decision support tools directly to end users in agriculture through compact and deployable systems. This work addresses (i) generating a structured insect information dataset, and (ii) adapting a lightweight LLM model ($\leq$ 7B) by fine tuning it for edge device uses in agricultural pest management. The textual data collection was done by reviewing and collecting information from available pest databases and published manuscripts on nine selected pest species. These structured reports were then reviewed and validated by a domain expert. From these reports, we constructed Q/A pairs to support model training and evaluation. A LoRA-based fine-tuning approach was applied to multiple lightweight LLMs and evaluated. Initial evaluation shows that Mistral 7B achieves an 88.9\% pass rate on the domain-specific Q/A task, substantially outperforming Qwen 2.5 7B (63.9\%), and LLaMA 3.1 8B (58.7\%). Notably, Mistral demonstrates higher semantic alignment (embedding similarity: 0.865) despite lower lexical overlap (BLEU: 0.097), indicating that semantic understanding and robust reasoning are more predictive of task success than surface-level conformity in specialized domains. By combining expert organized data, well-structured Q/A pairs, semantic quality control, and efficient model adaptation, this work contributes towards providing support for farmer facing agricultural decision support tools and demonstrates the feasibility of deploying compact, high-performing language models for practical field-level pest management guidance.

zh.title: AgriPestDatabase-v1.0：用于训练农业大型语言模型的结构化昆虫数据集
zh.summary: AgriPestDatabase和微调的7B语言模型为连接有限的农民提供离线害虫管理决策支持。Mistral 7B在农业问答任务上达到88.9%的准确率，展示了为服务不足的农村农业社区实际部署人工智能工具的能力。

fr.title: AgriPestDatabase-v1.0 : Un ensemble de données d'insectes structuré pour former un grand modèle de langage agricole
fr.summary: AgriPestDatabase et les modèles de langage 7B affinés offrent un soutien décisionnel pour la gestion des ravageurs hors ligne aux agriculteurs ayant une connectivité limitée. Mistral 7B atteint une précision de 88,9% sur les tâches de questions-réponses agricoles, démontrant le déploiement pratique des outils d'IA pour les communautés agricoles rurales mal desservies.

de.title: AgriPestDatabase-v1.0: Ein strukturierter Insektendatensatz zum Trainieren eines großen landwirtschaftlichen Sprachmodells
de.summary: AgriPestDatabase und feinabgestimmte 7B-Sprachmodelle ermöglichen Offline-Unterstützung für die Schädlingsbekämpfung für Landwirte mit begrenzter Konnektivität. Mistral 7B erreicht 88,9% Genauigkeit bei landwirtschaftlichen Frage-Antwort-Aufgaben und demonstriert die praktische Bereitstellung von KI-Tools für unterversorgten ländlichen landwirtschaftlichen Gemeinschaften.

es.title: AgriPestDatabase-v1.0: Un conjunto de datos de insectos estructurado para entrenar un modelo de lenguaje agrícola grande
es.summary: AgriPestDatabase y modelos de lenguaje 7B ajustados proporcionan soporte de decisiones de control de plagas sin conexión para agricultores con conectividad limitada. Mistral 7B logra una precisión del 88,9% en tareas de preguntas y respuestas agrícolas, demostrando la implementación práctica de herramientas de IA para comunidades agrícolas rurales desatendidas.

title: ABSTRAL: Automatic Design of Multi-Agent Systems Through Iterative Refinement and Topology Optimization
summary: ABSTRAL automatically designs multi-agent system architectures by iteratively refining natural language documents, discovering efficient task decompositions and specialist roles. The framework achieves 70% validation accuracy on complex banking tasks while producing inspectable design rationale.
content:
arXiv:2603.22791v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: How should multi-agent systems be designed, and can that design knowledge be captured in a form that is inspectable, revisable, and transferable? We introduce ABSTRAL, a framework that treats MAS architecture as an evolving natural-language document, an artifact refined through contrastive trace analysis. Three findings emerge. First, we provide a precise measurement of the multi-agent coordination tax: under fixed turn budgets, ensembles achieve only 26% turn efficiency, with 66% of tasks exhausting the limit, yet still improve over single-agent baselines by discovering parallelizable task decompositions. Second, design knowledge encoded in documents transfers: topology reasoning and role templates learned on one domain provide a head start on new domains, with transferred seeds matching coldstart iteration 3 performance in a single iteration. Third, contrastive trace analysis discovers specialist roles absent from any initial design, a capability no prior system demonstrates. On SOPBench (134 bank tasks, deterministic oracle), ABSTRAL reaches 70% validation / 65.96% test pass rate with a GPT-4o backbone. We release the converged documents as inspectable design rationale.

zh.title: ABSTRAL：通过迭代细化和拓扑优化自动设计多代理系统
zh.summary: ABSTRAL通过迭代细化自然语言文档来自动设计多代理系统架构，发现有效的任务分解和专家角色。该框架在复杂银行任务上达到70%的验证准确率，同时生成可检查的设计理由。

fr.title: ABSTRAL : Conception automatique de systèmes multi-agents par raffinement itératif et optimisation topologique
fr.summary: ABSTRAL conçoit automatiquement les architectures de systèmes multi-agents en affinant itérativement les documents en langage naturel, découvrant des décompositions efficaces des tâches et des rôles spécialisés. Le cadre atteint une précision de validation de 70% sur les tâches bancaires complexes tout en produisant une justification de conception inspectable.

de.title: ABSTRAL: Automatisches Design von Multi-Agent-Systemen durch iterative Verfeinerung und Topologie-Optimierung
de.summary: ABSTRAL entwirft automatisch Multi-Agent-Systemarchitekturen durch iterative Verfeinerung natürlichsprachiger Dokumente und entdeckt effiziente Aufgabenzerlegungen und Spezialrollen. Das Framework erreicht 70% Validierungsgenauigkeit bei komplexen Bankaufgaben und produziert nachvollziehbare Designbegründungen.

es.title: ABSTRAL: Diseño automático de sistemas multiagente mediante refinamiento iterativo y optimización topológica
