es.summary: ABSTRAL diseña automáticamente arquitecturas de sistemas multiagente refinando iterativamente documentos en lenguaje natural, descubriendo descomposiciones eficientes de tareas y roles especializados. El marco logra una precisión de validación del 70% en tareas bancarias complejas mientras produce una justificación de diseño inspectable.

title: Reliable Classroom AI via Neuro-Symbolic Multimodal Reasoning
summary: NSCR proposes a neuro-symbolic framework that combines AI perception with verifiable reasoning and governance for classroom analytics on engagement and instruction quality. The approach emphasizes interpretability, privacy, and calibration to make AI deployment in sensitive educational settings trustworthy.
content:
arXiv:2603.22793v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Classroom AI is rapidly expanding from low-level perception toward higher-level judgments about engagement, confusion, collaboration, and instructional quality. Yet classrooms are among the hardest real-world settings for multimodal vision: they are multi-party, noisy, privacy-sensitive, pedagogically diverse, and often multilingual. In this paper, we argue that classroom AI should be treated as a critical domain, where raw predictive accuracy is insufficient unless predictions are accompanied by verifiable evidence, calibrated uncertainty, and explicit deployment guardrails. We introduce NSCR, a neuro-symbolic framework that decomposes classroom analytics into four layers: perceptual grounding, symbolic abstraction, executable reasoning, and governance. NSCR adapts recent ideas from symbolic fact extraction and verifiable code generation to multimodal educational settings, enabling classroom observations from video, audio, ASR, and contextual metadata to be converted into typed facts and then composed by executable rules, programs, and policy constraints. Beyond the system design, we contribute a benchmark and evaluation protocol organized around five tasks: classroom state inference, discourse-grounded event linking, temporal early warning, collaboration analysis, and multilingual classroom reasoning. We further specify reliability metrics centered on abstention, calibration, robustness, construct alignment, and human usefulness. The paper does not report new empirical results; its contribution is a concrete framework and evaluation agenda intended to support more interpretable, privacy-aware, and pedagogically grounded multimodal AI for classrooms.

zh.title: 通过神经符号多模态推理实现可靠的课堂人工智能
zh.summary: NSCR提出了一个神经符号框架，将人工智能感知与可验证的推理和治理结合起来，用于课堂参与度和教学质量分析。该方法强调可解释性、隐私性和校准，以使人工智能在敏感教育环境中的部署值得信赖。

fr.title: L'IA de salle de classe fiable via le raisonnement multimodal neuro-symbolique
fr.summary: NSCR propose un cadre neuro-symbolique qui combine la perception de l'IA avec un raisonnement vérifiable et une gouvernance pour l'analyse de salle de classe sur l'engagement et la qualité de l'enseignement. L'approche met l'accent sur l'interprétabilité, la confidentialité et l'étalonnage pour rendre le déploiement de l'IA dans les environnements éducatifs sensibles fiable.

de.title: Zuverlässige Klassenzimmer-KI durch neuro-symbolisches multimodales Denken
de.summary: NSCR schlägt ein neuro-symbolisches Framework vor, das KI-Wahrnehmung mit überprüfbarem Denken und Governance für Klassenzimmeranalytik zu Engagement und Unterrichtsqualität verbindet. Der Ansatz betont Interpretierbarkeit, Datenschutz und Kalibrierung, um die KI-Bereitstellung in sensiblen Bildungsumgebungen vertrauenswürdig zu machen.

es.title: IA de aula confiable a través del razonamiento multimodal neuro-simbólico
es.summary: NSCR propone un marco neuro-simbólico que combina la percepción de IA con razonamiento verificable y gobernanza para análisis de aula sobre compromiso y calidad de instrucción. El enfoque enfatiza la interpretabilidad, privacidad y calibración para hacer que el despliegue de IA en entornos educativos sensibles sea confiable.

title: Learning What Matters Now: Dynamic Preference Inference under Contextual Shifts
summary: Dynamic Preference Inference (DPI) enables AI agents to learn and adapt to changing user priorities as circumstances shift rather than assuming fixed preferences. Testing shows the approach achieves better post-shift performance than static baselines by modeling realistic human preference changes.
content:
arXiv:2603.22813v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Humans often juggle multiple, sometimes conflicting objectives and shift their priorities as circumstances change, rather than following a fixed objective function. In contrast, most computational decision-making and multi-objective RL methods assume static preference weights or a known scalar reward. In this work, we study sequential decision-making problem when these preference weights are unobserved latent variables that drift with context. Specifically, we propose Dynamic Preference Inference (DPI), a cognitively inspired framework in which an agent maintains a probabilistic belief over preference weights, updates this belief from recent interaction, and conditions its policy on inferred preferences. We instantiate DPI as a variational preference inference module trained jointly with a preference-conditioned actor-critic, using vector-valued returns as evidence about latent trade-offs. In queueing, maze, and multi-objective continuous-control environments with event-driven changes in objectives, DPI adapts its inferred preferences to new regimes and achieves higher post-shift performance than fixed-weight and heuristic envelope baselines.

zh.title: 学习现在重要的东西：情境转变下的动态偏好推断
zh.summary: 动态偏好推断（DPI）使AI代理能够学习和适应不断变化的用户优先级，而不是假设固定的偏好。测试表明，通过对现实人类偏好变化的建模，该方法比静态基线在转变后的性能上表现更好。

fr.title: Apprendre ce qui compte maintenant : Inférence dynamique des préférences sous les changements contextuels
fr.summary: L'inférence dynamique des préférences (DPI) permet aux agents IA d'apprendre et de s'adapter aux priorités changeantes de l'utilisateur au lieu de supposer des préférences fixes. Les tests montrent que l'approche réalise une meilleure performance post-décalage que les baselines statiques en modélisant les changements réalistes de préférences humaines.

de.title: Lernen, was jetzt wichtig ist: Dynamische Präferenzinferenz unter kontextuellen Verschiebungen
de.summary: Dynamische Präferenzinferenz (DPI) ermöglicht es KI-Agenten, sich an verändernde Benutzerpriorität anzupassen, anstatt feste Präferenzen anzunehmen. Tests zeigen, dass der Ansatz durch die Modellierung realistischer menschlicher Präferenzänderungen eine bessere Leistung nach der Verschiebung als statische Baselines erreicht.

es.title: Aprender qué importa ahora: Inferencia dinámica de preferencias bajo cambios contextuales
es.summary: La Inferencia Dinámica de Preferencias (DPI) permite que los agentes de IA aprendan y se adapten a las prioridades cambiantes del usuario en lugar de asumir preferencias fijas. Las pruebas muestran que el enfoque logra un mejor desempeño posterior al cambio que las líneas base estáticas al modelar cambios realistas de preferencias humanas.

title: Empirical Comparison of Agent Communication Protocols for Task Orchestration
summary: Researchers conduct the first systematic benchmark comparing multi-agent communication protocols, measuring trade-offs in response time, cost, and error recovery across tool-integration-only, delegation, and hybrid architectures. The results provide empirical guidance for enterprise multi-agent deployment decisions.
content:
arXiv:2603.22823v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Context. Nowadays, artificial intelligence agent systems are transforming from single-tool interactions to complex multi-agent orchestrations. As a result, two competing communication protocols have emerged: a tool integration protocol that standardizes how agents invoke external tools, and an inter-agent delegation protocol that enables autonomous agents to discover and delegate tasks to one another. Despite widespread industry adoption by dozens of enterprise partners, no empirical comparison of these protocols exists in the literature. Objective. The goal of this work is to develop the first systematic benchmark comparing tool-integration-only, multi-agent delegation, and hybrid architectures across standardized queries at three complexity levels, and to quantify the trade-offs in response time, context window consumption, monetary cost, error recovery, and implementation complexity.

zh.title: 任务编排的代理通信协议的实证比较
zh.summary: 研究人员进行了首次系统基准测试，比较多代理通信协议，测量工具集成、委派和混合架构中响应时间、成本和错误恢复的权衡。结果为企业多代理部署决策提供了实证指导。

fr.title: Comparaison empirique des protocoles de communication des agents pour l'orchestration des tâches
fr.summary: Les chercheurs mènent le premier benchmark systématique comparant les protocoles de communication multi-agents, mesurant les compromis en temps de réponse, coût et récupération d'erreur sur les architectures d'intégration d'outils, de délégation et hybride. Les résultats fournissent des conseils empiriques pour les décisions de déploiement multi-agents d'entreprise.

de.title: Empirischer Vergleich von Agent-Kommunikationsprotokollen für Task-Orchestrierung
de.summary: Forscher führen den ersten systematischen Benchmark durch, der Multi-Agent-Kommunikationsprotokolle vergleicht und Kompromisse bei Antwortzeit, Kosten und Fehlerwiederherstellung über reine Tool-Integrations-, Delegations- und Hybridarchitekturen hinweg misst. Die Ergebnisse bieten empirische Anleitung für Enterprise-Multi-Agent-Bereitstellungsentscheidungen.

es.title: Comparación empírica de protocolos de comunicación de agentes para orquestación de tareas
es.summary: Los investigadores realizan el primer análisis comparativo sistemático de protocolos de comunicación multi-agente, midiendo compensaciones en tiempo de respuesta, costo y recuperación de errores en arquitecturas solo integración de herramientas, delegación e híbrida. Los resultados proporcionan orientación empírica para decisiones de implementación multi-agente empresarial.

title: Improving Safety Alignment via Balanced Direct Preference Optimization
summary: B-DPO improves upon Direct Preference Optimization for safety training by addressing imbalanced learning across preference pairs through adaptive modulation. The method enhances LLM safety capabilities while maintaining competitive general performance across standard benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22829v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: With the rapid development and widespread application of Large Language Models (LLMs), their potential safety risks have attracted widespread attention. Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) has been adopted to enhance the safety performance of LLMs. As a simple and effective alternative to RLHF, Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is widely used for safety alignment. However, safety alignment still suffers from severe overfitting, which limits its actual performance. This paper revisits the overfitting phenomenon from the perspective of the model's comprehension of the training data. We find that the Imbalanced Preference Comprehension phenomenon exists between responses in preference pairs, which compromises the model's safety performance. To address this, we propose Balanced Direct Preference Optimization (B-DPO), which adaptively modulates optimization strength between preferred and dispreferred responses based on mutual information. A series of experimental results show that B-DPO can enhance the safety capability while maintaining the competitive general capabilities of LLMs on various mainstream benchmarks compared to state-of-the-art methods. \color{red}{Warning: This paper contains examples of harmful texts, and reader discretion is recommended.

zh.title: 通过平衡直接偏好优化改进安全对齐
zh.summary: B-DPO通过自适应调制解决偏好对之间的不平衡学习，改进了直接偏好优化以进行安全训练。该方法增强了LLM安全能力，同时保持在标准基准上的竞争性通用性能。

fr.title: Amélioration de l'alignement de sécurité via l'optimisation des préférences directes équilibrées
fr.summary: B-DPO améliore l'optimisation directe des préférences pour l'entraînement de sécurité en abordant l'apprentissage déséquilibré entre les paires de préférences par modulation adaptative. La méthode améliore les capacités de sécurité du LLM tout en maintenant une performance générale compétitive sur les benchmarks standard.

de.title: Verbesserung der Sicherheitsausrichtung durch ausgewogene direkte Präferenzoptimierung
de.summary: B-DPO verbessert die direkte Präferenzoptimierung für Sicherheitstraining durch Behebung des Ungleichgewichts beim Lernen über Präferenzpaare durch adaptive Modulation. Die Methode verbessert die Sicherheitsfähigkeiten des LLM, während die wettbewerbsfähige allgemeine Leistung über Standard-Benchmarks erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Mejora de la alineación de seguridad mediante optimización de preferencias directas equilibrada
es.summary: B-DPO mejora la optimización directa de preferencias para entrenamiento de seguridad abordando el aprendizaje desequilibrado entre pares de preferencias mediante modulación adaptativa. El método mejora las capacidades de seguridad del LLM manteniendo un desempeño general competitivo en benchmarks estándar.

title: PhySe-RPO: Physics and Semantics Guided Relative Policy Optimization for Diffusion-Based Surgical Smoke Removal
summary: PhySe-RPO uses reinforcement learning with physics-guided and semantic rewards to remove surgical smoke from intraoperative video, improving visibility of anatomical structures. The approach avoids requiring extensive paired training data while maintaining clinical interpretability.
content:
arXiv:2603.22844v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Surgical smoke severely degrades intraoperative video quality, obscuring anatomical structures and limiting surgical perception. Existing learning-based desmoking approaches rely on scarce paired supervision and deterministic restoration pipelines, making it difficult to perform exploration or reinforcement-driven refinement under real surgical conditions. We propose PhySe-RPO, a diffusion restoration framework optimized through Physics- and Semantics-Guided Relative Policy Optimization. The core idea is to transform deterministic restoration into a stochastic policy, enabling trajectory-level exploration and critic-free updates via group-relative optimization. A physics-guided reward imposes illumination and color consistency, while a visual-concept semantic reward learned from CLIP-based surgical concepts promotes smoke-free and anatomically coherent restoration. Together with a reference-free perceptual constraint, PhySe-RPO produces results that are physically consistent, semantically faithful, and clinically interpretable across synthetic and real robotic surgical datasets, providing a principled route to robust diffusion-based restoration under limited paired supervision.

zh.title: PhySe-RPO：物理和语义引导的相对策略优化在基于扩散的手术烟雾移除中的应用
zh.summary: PhySe-RPO使用具有物理引导和语义奖励的强化学习从术中视频中去除手术烟雾，改善解剖结构的可见性。该方法避免了需要大量配对训练数据，同时保持临床可解释性。

fr.title: PhySe-RPO : Optimisation de politique relative guidée par la physique et la sémantique pour l'élimination de la fumée chirurgicale basée sur la diffusion
fr.summary: PhySe-RPO utilise l'apprentissage par renforcement avec des récompenses guidées par la physique et la sémantique pour éliminer la fumée chirurgicale des vidéos peropératoires, améliorant la visibilité des structures anatomiques. L'approche évite de nécessiter d'importantes données d'entraînement appariées tout en maintenant l'interprétabilité clinique.

de.title: PhySe-RPO: Durch Physik und Semantik geleitete relative Richtlinienoptimierung zur diffusionsbasierten Entfernung von Operationsdampf
de.summary: PhySe-RPO nutzt verstärktes Lernen mit physik- und semantikgeleiteten Belohnungen, um Operationsdampf aus intraoperativen Videos zu entfernen und die Sichtbarkeit anatomischer Strukturen zu verbessern. Der Ansatz vermeidet die Notwendigkeit umfangreicher gepaarter Trainingsdaten und behält gleichzeitig klinische Interpretierbarkeit.

es.title: PhySe-RPO: Optimización de Política Relativa Guiada por Física y Semántica para la Eliminación de Humo Quirúrgico Basada en Difusión
es.summary: PhySe-RPO utiliza aprendizaje por refuerzo con recompensas guiadas por física y semántica para eliminar el humo quirúrgico de videos intraoperatorios, mejorando la visibilidad de estructuras anatómicas. El enfoque evita requerir amplios datos de entrenamiento emparejados manteniendo la interpretabilidad clínica.

title: CoMaTrack: Competitive Multi-Agent Game-Theoretic Tracking with Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: CoMaTrack trains embodied AI agents in competitive multi-agent settings to improve visual tracking robustness and adaptive planning. A 3B model trained with competitive dynamics surpasses previous 7B models on standard tracking benchmarks, demonstrating the value of adversarial training.
content:
arXiv:2603.22846v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Embodied Visual Tracking (EVT), a core dynamic task in embodied intelligence, requires an agent to precisely follow a language-specified target. Yet most existing methods rely on single-agent imitation learning, suffering from costly expert data and limited generalization due to static training environments. Inspired by competition-driven capability evolution, we propose CoMaTrack, a competitive game-theoretic multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that trains agents in a dynamic adversarial setting with competitive subtasks, yielding stronger adaptive planning and interference-resilient strategies. We further introduce CoMaTrack-Bench, the first benchmark for competitive EVT, featuring game scenarios between a tracker and adaptive opponents across diverse environments and instructions, enabling standardized robustness evaluation under active adversarial interactions. Experiments show that CoMaTrack achieves state-of-the-art results on both standard benchmarks and CoMaTrack-Bench. Notably, a 3B VLM trained with our framework surpasses previous single-agent imitation learning methods based on 7B models on the challenging EVT-Bench, achieving 92.1% in STT, 74.2% in DT, and 57.5% in AT. The benchmark code will be available at https://github.com/wlqcode/CoMaTrack-Bench

zh.title: CoMaTrack：基于视觉-语言-行动模型的竞争性多智能体博弈论跟踪
zh.summary: CoMaTrack在竞争性多智能体设置中训练具身AI智能体，以提高视觉跟踪的鲁棒性和自适应规划。一个用竞争动力学训练的3B模型在标准跟踪基准上超越了之前的7B模型，展示了对抗性训练的价值。

fr.title: CoMaTrack : Suivi Compétitif Multi-Agent Théorique du Jeu avec des Modèles Vision-Langage-Action
fr.summary: CoMaTrack entraîne des agents IA incarnés dans des environnements multi-agents compétitifs pour améliorer la robustesse du suivi visuel et la planification adaptative. Un modèle de 3B entraîné avec une dynamique compétitive surpasse les modèles de 7B précédents sur les benchmarks de suivi standard, démontrant la valeur de l'entraînement adversarial.

de.title: CoMaTrack: Wettbewerbsfähiges spieltheoretisches Multi-Agent-Tracking mit Vision-Language-Action-Modellen
de.summary: CoMaTrack trainiert verkörperte KI-Agenten in wettbewerbsfähigen Multi-Agent-Umgebungen, um die Robustheit des visuellen Trackings und adaptive Planung zu verbessern. Ein 3B-Modell, das mit wettbewerbsfähiger Dynamik trainiert wird, übertrifft frühere 7B-Modelle bei Standard-Tracking-Benchmarks und zeigt den Wert von adversarial Training.

es.title: CoMaTrack: Seguimiento Multiagente Competitivo Teórico de Juegos con Modelos de Visión-Lenguaje-Acción
es.summary: CoMaTrack entrena agentes de IA incorporados en configuraciones multiagente competitivas para mejorar la robustez del seguimiento visual y la planificación adaptativa. Un modelo de 3B entrenado con dinámicas competitivas supera a los modelos de 7B anteriores en pruebas estándar de seguimiento, demostrando el valor del entrenamiento adversarial.

title: Chain-of-Authorization: Internalizing Authorization into Large Language Models via Reasoning Trajectories
summary: Chain-of-Authorization embeds fine-grained permission awareness into LLM reasoning, requiring models to reason through authorization logic before accessing sensitive data. The approach addresses security risks from unauthorized data access and manipulation in AI systems better than external-only defenses.
content:
arXiv:2603.22869v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become core cognitive components in modern artificial intelligence (AI) systems, combining internal knowledge with external context to perform complex tasks. However, LLMs typically treat all accessible data indiscriminately, lacking inherent awareness of knowledge ownership and access boundaries. This deficiency heightens risks of sensitive data leakage and adversarial manipulation, potentially enabling unauthorized system access and severe security crises. Existing protection strategies rely on rigid, uniform defense that prevent dynamic authorization. Structural isolation methods faces scalability bottlenecks, while prompt guidance methods struggle with fine-grained permissions distinctions. Here, we propose the Chain-of-Authorization (CoA) framework, a secure training and reasoning paradigm that internalizes authorization logic into LLMs' core capabilities. Unlike passive external defneses, CoA restructures the model's information flow: it embeds permission context at input and requires generating explicit authorization reasoning trajectory that includes resource review, identity resolution, and decision-making stages before final response. Through supervised fine-tuning on data covering various authorization status, CoA integrates policy execution with task responses, making authorization a causal prerequisite for substantive responses. Extensive evaluations show that CoA not only maintains comparable utility in authorized scenarios but also overcomes the cognitive confusion when permissions mismatches. It exhibits high rejection rates against various unauthorized and adversarial access. This mechanism leverages LLMs' reasoning capability to perform dynamic authorization, using natural language understanding as a proactive security mechanism for deploying reliable LLMs in modern AI systems.

zh.title: 授权链：通过推理轨迹将授权内部化到大型语言模型中
zh.summary: Chain-of-Authorization将细粒度权限意识嵌入到LLM推理中，要求模型在访问敏感数据前通过授权逻辑进行推理。该方法比仅外部防御更好地解决了AI系统中未授权数据访问和操纵的安全风险。

fr.title: Chaîne d'Autorisation : Internaliser l'Autorisation dans les Modèles de Langage de Grande Taille via les Trajectoires de Raisonnement
fr.summary: Chain-of-Authorization intègre une sensibilisation aux permissions fine-grained dans le raisonnement des LLM, obligeant les modèles à raisonner à travers la logique d'autorisation avant d'accéder aux données sensibles. L'approche aborde mieux les risques de sécurité liés à l'accès non autorisé aux données et à la manipulation dans les systèmes d'IA que les défenses externes uniquement.

de.title: Autorisierungskette: Autorisierung in großen Sprachmodellen durch Reasoning-Trajektorien internalisieren
de.summary: Chain-of-Authorization bringt feingranulares Autorisierungsbewusstsein in LLM-Reasoning ein und erfordert, dass Modelle Autorisierungslogik durchdenken, bevor sie auf sensible Daten zugreifen. Der Ansatz berücksichtigt Sicherheitsrisiken durch unbefugte Datenzugriffe und Manipulation in KI-Systemen besser als nur externe Schutzmaßnahmen.

es.title: Cadena de Autorización: Internalizando la Autorización en Modelos de Lenguaje de Gran Tamaño a través de Trayectorias de Razonamiento
es.summary: Chain-of-Authorization incrusta conciencia de permisos de grano fino en el razonamiento de LLM, requiriendo que los modelos razonen a través de la lógica de autorización antes de acceder a datos sensibles. El enfoque aborda mejor los riesgos de seguridad del acceso no autorizado a datos y manipulación en sistemas de IA que las defensas solo externas.

title: Dynamical Systems Theory Behind a Hierarchical Reasoning Model
summary: The Contraction Mapping Model (CMM) uses mathematical differential equations for AI reasoning, outperforming much larger models while using only 5M parameters. CMM achieves 93.7% accuracy on complex tasks, proving mathematical rigor can replace raw scaling.
content:
arXiv:2603.22871v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Current large language models (LLMs) primarily rely on linear sequence generation and massive parameter counts, yet they severely struggle with complex algorithmic reasoning. While recent reasoning architectures, such as the Hierarchical Reasoning Model (HRM) and Tiny Recursive Model (TRM), demonstrate that compact recursive networks can tackle these tasks, their training dynamics often lack rigorous mathematical guarantees, leading to instability and representational collapse. We propose the Contraction Mapping Model (CMM), a novel architecture that reformulates discrete recursive reasoning into continuous Neural Ordinary and Stochastic Differential Equations (NODEs/NSDEs). By explicitly enforcing the convergence of the latent phase point to a stable equilibrium state and mitigating feature collapse with a hyperspherical repulsion loss, the CMM provides a mathematically grounded and highly stable reasoning engine. On the Sudoku-Extreme benchmark, a 5M-parameter CMM achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 93.7 %, outperforming the 27M-parameter HRM (55.0 %) and 5M-parameter TRM (87.4 %). Remarkably, even when aggressively compressed to an ultra-tiny footprint of just 0.26M parameters, the CMM retains robust predictive power, achieving 85.4 % on Sudoku-Extreme and 82.2 % on the Maze benchmark. These results establish a new frontier for extreme parameter efficiency, proving that mathematically rigorous latent dynamics can effectively replace brute-force scaling in artificial reasoning.

zh.title: 动力系统理论在分层推理模型中的应用
zh.summary: 收缩映射模型(CMM)使用数学微分方程进行AI推理，仅使用500万参数就超越了更大的模型。CMM在复杂任务上达到93.7%的准确度，证明数学严谨性可以替代原始规模扩展。

fr.title: Théorie des Systèmes Dynamiques Derrière un Modèle de Raisonnement Hiérarchique
fr.summary: Le Modèle de Contraction (CMM) utilise des équations différentielles mathématiques pour le raisonnement IA, surpassant les modèles beaucoup plus grands avec seulement 5 millions de paramètres. CMM atteint une précision de 93,7% sur les tâches complexes, prouvant que la rigueur mathématique peut remplacer la mise à l'échelle brute.

de.title: Dynamische Systemtheorie hinter einem hierarchischen Reasoning-Modell
de.summary: Das Contraction Mapping Model (CMM) nutzt mathematische Differentialgleichungen für KI-Reasoning und übertrifft viel größere Modelle mit nur 5 Millionen Parametern. CMM erreicht 93,7% Genauigkeit bei komplexen Aufgaben und beweist, dass mathematische Strenge Raw Scaling ersetzen kann.

es.title: Teoría de Sistemas Dinámicos Detrás de un Modelo de Razonamiento Jerárquico
es.summary: El Modelo de Mapeo de Contracción (CMM) utiliza ecuaciones diferenciales matemáticas para el razonamiento de IA, superando a modelos mucho más grandes con solo 5 millones de parámetros. CMM logra una precisión del 93,7% en tareas complejas, demostrando que el rigor matemático puede reemplazar el escalado bruto.

title: Continuous Optimization for Satisfiability Modulo Theories on Linear Real Arithmetic
summary: FourierSMT introduces a parallelizable gradient-based framework for satisfiability problems, achieving 8-fold speedups over traditional solvers on large-scale industrial tasks. The approach enables GPU-based optimization for hardware verification and design automation.
content:
arXiv:2603.22877v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Efficient solutions for satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) are integral in industrial applications such as hardware verification and design automation. Existing approaches are predominantly based on conflict-driven clause learning, which is structurally difficult to parallelize and therefore scales poorly. In this work, we introduce FourierSMT as a scalable and highly parallelizable continuous-variable optimization framework for SMT. We generalize the Walsh-Fourier expansion (WFE), called extended WFE (xWFE), from the Boolean domain to a mixed Boolean-real domain, which allows the use of gradient methods for SMT. This addresses the challenge of finding satisfying variable assignments to high-arity constraints by local updates of discrete variables. To reduce the evaluation complexity of xWFE, we present the extended binary decision diagram (xBDD) and map the constraints from xWFE to xBDDs. We then show that sampling the circuit-output probability (COP) of xBDDs under randomized rounding is equivalent to the expectation value of the xWFEs. This allows for efficient computation of the constraints. We show that the reduced problem is guaranteed to converge and preserves satisfiability, ensuring the soundness of the solutions. The framework is benchmarked for large-scale scheduling and placement problems with up to 10,000 variables and 700,000 constraints, achieving 8-fold speedups compared to state-of-the-art SMT solvers. These results pave the way for GPU-based optimization of SMTs with continuous systems.

zh.title: 线性实数算术可满足性模理论的连续优化
zh.summary: FourierSMT引入了一个可并行化的基于梯度的框架来解决可满足性问题，在大规模工业任务上比传统求解器快8倍。该方法为硬件验证和设计自动化启用了基于GPU的优化。

fr.title: Optimisation Continue pour la Satisfiabilité Modulo Théories sur l'Arithmétique Réelle Linéaire
fr.summary: FourierSMT introduit un cadre parallélisable basé sur le gradient pour les problèmes de satisfiabilité, atteignant des accélérations 8 fois supérieures aux solveurs traditionnels sur les tâches industrielles à grande échelle. L'approche permet l'optimisation basée sur GPU pour la vérification matérielle et l'automatisation de la conception.

de.title: Kontinuierliche Optimierung für Satisfiability Modulo Theories bei linearer reeller Arithmetik
de.summary: FourierSMT führt ein parallelisierbares gradientenbasiertes Framework für Erfüllbarkeitsprobleme ein und erreicht 8-fache Speedups gegenüber traditionellen Lösern bei großen Industrieaufgaben. Der Ansatz ermöglicht GPU-basierte Optimierung für Hardware-Verifikation und Designautomation.

es.title: Optimización Continua para Satisfacibilidad Módulo Teorías en Aritmética Real Lineal
es.summary: FourierSMT introduce un marco paralelizable basado en gradientes para problemas de satisfacibilidad, logrando aceleraciones 8 veces mayores que los solucionadores tradicionales en tareas industriales a gran escala. El enfoque permite optimización basada en GPU para verificación de hardware y automatización de diseño.

title: Separating Diagnosis from Control: Auditable Policy Adaptation in Agent-Based Simulations with LLM-Based Diagnostics
summary: A three-layer framework uses LLMs purely for diagnosis while keeping policy decisions traceable through deterministic rules, addressing elderly loneliness interventions. The approach outperforms black-box LLM control by 11.7% while maintaining full auditability.
content:
arXiv:2603.22904v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Mitigating elderly loneliness requires policy interventions that achieve both adaptability and auditability. Existing methods struggle to reconcile these objectives: traditional agent-based models suffer from static rigidity, while direct large language model (LLM) controllers lack essential traceability. This work proposes a three-layer framework that separates diagnosis from control to achieve both properties simultaneously. LLMs operate strictly as diagnostic instruments that assess population state and generate structured risk evaluations, while deterministic formulas with explicit bounds translate these assessments into traceable parameter updates. This separation ensures that every policy decision can be attributed to inspectable rules while maintaining adaptive response to emergent needs. We validate the framework through systematic ablation across five experimental conditions in elderly care simulation. Results demonstrate that explicit control rules outperform end-to-end black-box LLM approaches by 11.7\% while preserving full auditability, confirming that transparency need not compromise adaptive performance.

zh.title: 分离诊断与控制：基于代理模拟中的可审计策略适应与LLM诊断
zh.summary: 一个三层框架仅将LLM用于诊断，同时通过确定性规则保持策略决策的可追溯性，针对老年孤独干预。该方法比黑盒LLM控制的性能提高11.7%，同时保持完全的可审计性。

fr.title: Séparer le Diagnostic du Contrôle : Adaptation de Politique Auditable dans les Simulations Basées sur des Agents avec Diagnostique Basée sur les LLM
fr.summary: Un cadre à trois niveaux utilise les LLM uniquement pour le diagnostic tout en gardant les décisions politiques traçables par des règles déterministes, adressant les interventions contre la solitude des personnes âgées. L'approche surpasse le contrôle LLM de boîte noire de 11,7% tout en maintenant une auditabilité complète.

de.title: Diagnose von Kontrolle trennen: Überprüfbare Richtlinienanpassung in agentengestützten Simulationen mit LLM-basierter Diagnostik
de.summary: Ein dreischichtiges Framework verwendet LLMs rein zur Diagnose, während Richtlinienentscheidungen durch deterministische Regeln nachverfolgbar bleiben und sich auf Einsamkeitsinterventionen bei älteren Menschen konzentrieren. Der Ansatz übertrifft die Black-Box-LLM-Kontrolle um 11,7%, während volle Prüfbarkeit erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Separar Diagnóstico del Control: Adaptación Auditable de Políticas en Simulaciones Basadas en Agentes con Diagnósticos Basados en LLM
es.summary: Un marco de tres capas utiliza LLM puramente para el diagnóstico mientras mantiene las decisiones de política trazables a través de reglas deterministas, abordando intervenciones de soledad en adultos mayores. El enfoque supera el control LLM de caja negra en un 11,7% mientras mantiene una auditabilidad completa.

title: ProGRank: Probe-Gradient Reranking to Defend Dense-Retriever RAG from Corpus Poisoning
summary: ProGRank defends retrieval-augmented generation systems from corpus poisoning using probe gradients to identify malicious passages. This training-free retriever-side defense requires no retraining and maintains strong performance under adversarial attacks.
content:
arXiv:2603.22934v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves the reliability of large language model applications by grounding generation in retrieved evidence, but it also introduces a new attack surface: corpus poisoning. In this setting, an adversary injects or edits passages so that they are ranked into the Top-$K$ results for target queries and then affect downstream generation. Existing defences against corpus poisoning often rely on content filtering, auxiliary models, or generator-side reasoning, which can make deployment more difficult. We propose ProGRank, a post hoc, training-free retriever-side defence for dense-retriever RAG. ProGRank stress-tests each query--passage pair under mild randomized perturbations and extracts probe gradients from a small fixed parameter subset of the retriever. From these signals, it derives two instability signals, representational consistency and dispersion risk, and combines them with a score gate in a reranking step. ProGRank preserves the original passage content, requires no retraining, and also supports a surrogate-based variant when the deployed retriever is unavailable. Extensive experiments across three datasets, three dense retriever backbones, representative corpus poisoning attacks, and both retrieval-stage and end-to-end settings show that ProGRank provides stronger defence performance and a favorable robustness--utility trade-off. It also remains competitive under adaptive evasive attacks.

zh.title: ProGRank：探针梯度重新排序以防御密集检索RAG免受语料库投毒
zh.summary: ProGRank通过探针梯度识别恶意段落，防御检索增强生成系统免受语料库投毒。这种无需训练的检索器端防御无需重新训练，在对抗攻击下保持强大性能。

fr.title: ProGRank : Réordonnancement par gradient de sonde pour défendre RAG à récupérateur dense contre l'empoisonnement du corpus
fr.summary: ProGRank défend les systèmes de génération augmentée par récupération contre l'empoisonnement du corpus en utilisant des gradients de sonde pour identifier les passages malveillants. Cette défense côté récupérateur sans formation ne nécessite pas de réentraînement et maintient des performances solides sous les attaques adversariales.

de.title: ProGRank: Sonden-Gradienten-Reranking zur Verteidigung von dichtem Abruf-RAG vor Corpus-Vergiftung
de.summary: ProGRank verteidigt Retrieval-Augmented-Generation-Systeme vor Corpus-Vergiftung mit Sonden-Gradienten zur Identifikation bösartiger Passagen. Diese trainingsfreie Retriever-seitige Verteidigung erfordert kein Neutraining und behält starke Leistung unter gegnerischen Angriffen bei.

es.title: ProGRank: Reordenamiento de Gradiente de Sonda para Defender RAG de Recuperador Denso de Envenenamiento de Corpus
es.summary: ProGRank defiende los sistemas de generación aumentada por recuperación del envenenamiento de corpus utilizando gradientes de sonda para identificar pasajes maliciosos. Esta defensa del lado del recuperador sin entrenamiento no requiere reentrenamiento y mantiene un rendimiento sólido bajo ataques adversariales.

title: Ran Score: a LLM-based Evaluation Score for Radiology Report Generation
summary: Ran Score is an LLM-based evaluation metric for chest X-ray reports that handles low-prevalence abnormalities and linguistic nuances like negation and ambiguity. The clinician-guided framework improved evaluation accuracy by over 15 percentage points.
content:
arXiv:2603.22935v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Chest X-ray report generation and automated evaluation are limited by poor recognition of low-prevalence abnormalities and inadequate handling of clinically important language, including negation and ambiguity. We develop a clinician-guided framework combining human expertise and large language models for multi-label finding extraction from free-text chest X-ray reports and use it to define Ran Score, a finding-level metric for report evaluation. Using three non-overlapping MIMIC-CXR-EN cohorts from a public chest X-ray dataset and an independent ChestX-CN validation cohort, we optimize prompts, establish radiologist-derived reference labels and evaluate report generation models. The optimized framework improves the macro-averaged score from 0.753 to 0.956 on the MIMIC-CXR-EN development cohort, exceeds the CheXbert benchmark by 15.7 percentage points on directly comparable labels, and shows robust generalization on the ChestX-CN validation cohort. Here we show that clinician-guided prompt optimization improves agreement with a radiologist-derived reference standard and that Ran Score enables finding-level evaluation of report fidelity, particularly for low-prevalence abnormalities.

zh.title: Ran Score：基于LLM的放射学报告生成评估分数
zh.summary: Ran Score是一种基于LLM的胸部X光报告评估指标，可处理低患病率异常以及否定和歧义等语言细微差别。这个临床医生指导的框架使评估准确度提高了15个百分点以上。

fr.title: Ran Score : Un score d'évaluation basé sur LLM pour la génération de rapports radiologiques
fr.summary: Ran Score est une métrique d'évaluation basée sur LLM pour les rapports de radiographie thoracique qui gère les anomalies de faible prévalence et les nuances linguistiques telles que la négation et l'ambiguïté. Le cadre guidé par les cliniciens a amélioré la précision de l'évaluation de plus de 15 points de pourcentage.

de.title: Ran Score: Ein auf LLM basierender Bewertungsscore für die Generierung von Radiologieberichten
de.summary: Ran Score ist eine auf LLM basierende Bewertungsmetrik für Röntgenaufnahmen der Brust, die Anomalien mit niedriger Prävalenz und sprachliche Nuancen wie Negation und Mehrdeutigkeit behandelt. Das von Klinikern geleitete Framework verbesserte die Bewertungsgenauigkeit um über 15 Prozentpunkte.

es.title: Ran Score: Una puntuación de evaluación basada en LLM para la generación de informes radiológicos
es.summary: Ran Score es una métrica de evaluación basada en LLM para informes de radiografía de tórax que maneja anomalías de baja prevalencia y matices lingüísticos como negación y ambigüedad. El marco guiado por clínicos mejoró la precisión de la evaluación en más de 15 puntos porcentuales.

title: Optimizing Small Language Models for NL2SQL via Chain-of-Thought Fine-Tuning
summary: Small language models can be cost-effectively fine-tuned for natural language to SQL translation, with chain-of-thought training boosting accuracy to 54.5%. While still below large models, this achieves production-grade performance at significantly lower inference costs.
content:
arXiv:2603.22942v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Translating Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) remains a critical bottleneck for democratization of data in enterprises. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) like Gemini 2.5 and other LLMs have demonstrated impressive zero-shot capabilities, their high inference costs limit deployment at scale. This paper explores the efficacy of fine-tuning both large and small language models on NL2SQL tasks.
  Our research reveals a counter-intuitive scaling phenomenon. Fine-tuning large models (Gemini 2.5 Flash/Lite) on standard datasets yields negligible returns, often leading to overfitting on complex queries. Conversely, small models (Qwen) show significant gains. Fine-tuning improved the small model baseline from 36% to 45%, and further enriching the dataset with explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning surged accuracy to 54.5%(Fig 2). While this is still lower than the accuracy of large models like Gemini 2.5 , it does serve the business goal of significant cost reduction, latency in inference time and also meeting the business critical performance accuracy threshold.This paper demonstrates that transferring reasoning patterns enables compute-efficient smaller models to approach production-grade performance.

zh.title: 通过思维链微调优化NL2SQL小语言模型
zh.summary: 小型语言模型可以经济有效地进行微调以进行自然语言到SQL的转换，思维链训练将准确率提升到54.5%。虽然仍低于大型模型，但这以显著更低的推理成本实现了生产级性能。

fr.title: Optimisation des petits modèles de langage pour NL2SQL via le réglage fin à chaîne de pensée
fr.summary: Les petits modèles de langage peuvent être affinés de manière rentable pour la traduction du langage naturel en SQL, l'entraînement par chaîne de pensée augmentant la précision à 54,5 %. Bien que toujours inférieur aux grands modèles, cela réalise des performances de niveau production à des coûts d'inférence considérablement inférieur.

de.title: Optimierung kleiner Sprachmodelle für NL2SQL durch Chain-of-Thought-Feinabstimmung
de.summary: Kleine Sprachmodelle können kostengünstig für die Übersetzung von natürlicher Sprache in SQL optimiert werden, wobei das Chain-of-Thought-Training die Genauigkeit auf 54,5 % erhöht. Obwohl immer noch unter großen Modellen, erreicht dies produktionsreife Leistung bei erheblich niedrigeren Inferenzkosten.

es.title: Optimización de modelos de lenguaje pequeños para NL2SQL mediante ajuste fino en cadena de pensamiento
es.summary: Los modelos de lenguaje pequeños se pueden ajustar de forma rentable para la traducción de lenguaje natural a SQL, con el entrenamiento de cadena de pensamiento aumentando la precisión a 54,5%. Aunque aún está por debajo de los modelos grandes, esto logra un rendimiento de nivel de producción con costos de inferencia significativamente menores.

title: PersonalQ: Select, Quantize, and Serve Personalized Diffusion Models for Efficient Inference
summary: PersonalQ enables efficient serving of personalized text-to-image models through intent-aware checkpoint selection and trigger-aware quantization. The framework improves intent alignment and compression while maintaining generation fidelity for scalable deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.22943v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Personalized text-to-image generation lets users fine-tune diffusion models into repositories of concept-specific checkpoints, but serving these repositories efficiently is difficult for two reasons: natural-language requests are often ambiguous and can be misrouted to visually similar checkpoints, and standard post-training quantization can distort the fragile representations that encode personalized concepts. We present PersonalQ, a unified framework that connects checkpoint selection and quantization through a shared signal -- the checkpoint's trigger token. Check-in performs intent-aligned selection by combining intent-aware hybrid retrieval with LLM-based reranking over checkpoint context and asks a brief clarification question only when multiple intents remain plausible; it then rewrites the prompt by inserting the selected checkpoint's canonical trigger. Complementing this, Trigger-Aware Quantization (TAQ) applies trigger-aware mixed precision in cross-attention, preserving trigger-conditioned key/value rows (and their attention weights) while aggressively quantizing the remaining pathways for memory-efficient inference. Experiments show that PersonalQ improves intent alignment over retrieval and reranking baselines, while TAQ consistently offers a stronger compression-quality trade-off than prior diffusion PTQ methods, enabling scalable serving of personalized checkpoints without sacrificing fidelity.

zh.title: PersonalQ：选择、量化和服务个性化扩散模型以实现高效推理
zh.summary: PersonalQ通过意图感知检查点选择和触发器感知量化，实现个性化文本到图像模型的高效服务。该框架改进了意图对齐和压缩，同时为可扩展部署保持生成保真度。

fr.title: PersonalQ : Sélectionner, quantifier et servir des modèles de diffusion personnalisés pour une inférence efficace
fr.summary: PersonalQ permet un service efficace de modèles de texte à image personnalisés grâce à la sélection de points de contrôle consciente de l'intention et à la quantification consciente des déclencheurs. Le cadre améliore l'alignement des intentions et la compression tout en maintenant la fidélité de la génération pour un déploiement évolutif.

de.title: PersonalQ: Auswahl, Quantisierung und Bereitstellung personalisierter Diffusionsmodelle für effiziente Inferenz
de.summary: PersonalQ ermöglicht die effiziente Bereitstellung von personalisierten Text-zu-Bild-Modellen durch intentionsbewusste Checkpoint-Auswahl und triggerbewusste Quantisierung. Das Framework verbessert die Intentionsausrichtung und Kompression, während es die Generierungstreue für skalierbare Bereitstellung beibehält.

es.title: PersonalQ: Seleccionar, cuantizar y servir modelos de difusión personalizados para una inferencia eficiente
es.summary: PersonalQ permite el servicio eficiente de modelos de texto a imagen personalizados mediante selección de puntos de control consciente de la intención y cuantificación consciente de desencadenantes. El marco mejora la alineación de intenciones y la compresión mientras mantiene la fidelidad de generación para un despliegue escalable.

title: Where Experts Disagree, Models Fail: Detecting Implicit Legal Citations in French Court Decisions
summary: A study on implicit French Civil Code citations in court decisions reveals that model errors correlate with expert disagreement, particularly around boundaries between factual description and legal reasoning. Unsupervised ensemble methods achieve 76% precision for citation detection.
content:
arXiv:2603.22973v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Computational methods applied to legal scholarship hold the promise of analyzing law at scale. We start from a simple question: how often do courts implicitly apply statutory rules? This requires distinguishing legal reasoning from semantic similarity. We focus on implicit citation of the French Civil Code in first-instance court decisions and introduce a benchmark of 1,015 passage-article pairs annotated by three legal experts. We show that expert disagreement predicts model failures. Inter-annotator agreement is moderate ($\kappa$ = 0.33) with 43% of disagreements involving the boundary between factual description and legal reasoning. Our supervised ensemble achieves F1 = 0.70 (77% accuracy), but this figure conceals an asymmetry: 68% of false positives fall on the 33% of cases where the annotators disagreed. Despite these limits, reframing the task as top-k ranking and leveraging multi-model consensus yields 76% precision at k = 200 in an unsupervised setting. Moreover, the remaining false positives tend to surface legally ambiguous applications rather than obvious errors.

zh.title: 专家意见分歧时，模型失败：检测法国法院判决中的隐含法律引用
zh.summary: 关于法国民法典在法院判决中的隐含引用的研究表明，模型错误与专家意见分歧相关，特别是在事实描述和法律推理的边界处。无监督集合方法在引用检测中达到76%的精度。

fr.title: Où les experts sont en désaccord, les modèles échouent : Détection des citations légales implicites dans les décisions des tribunaux français
fr.summary: Une étude sur les citations implicites du Code civil français dans les décisions judiciaires révèle que les erreurs des modèles sont corrélées au désaccord entre les experts, particulièrement autour des limites entre la description factuelle et le raisonnement juridique. Les méthodes d'ensemble non supervisées atteignent 76% de précision pour la détection des citations.

de.title: Wo Experten uneinig sind, scheitern Modelle: Erkennung impliziter rechtlicher Zitate in französischen Gerichtsentscheidungen
de.summary: Eine Studie zu impliziten französischen Zivilgesetzbuch-Zitaten in Gerichtsentscheidungen zeigt, dass Modellfehler mit Expertenmeinungsverschiedenheiten korrelieren, besonders an den Grenzen zwischen faktischer Beschreibung und rechtlicher Begründung. Unüberwachte Ensemble-Methoden erreichen 76% Genauigkeit bei der Zitaterkennung.

es.title: Donde los expertos no están de acuerdo, los modelos fallan: Detección de citas legales implícitas en decisiones de tribunales franceses
es.summary: Un estudio sobre citas implícitas del Código Civil Francés en decisiones judiciales revela que los errores del modelo se correlacionan con el desacuerdo entre expertos, particularmente alrededor de los límites entre descripción fáctica y razonamiento legal. Los métodos de conjunto no supervisados logran una precisión del 76% para la detección de citas.

title: JFTA-Bench: Evaluate LLM's Ability of Tracking and Analyzing Malfunctions Using Fault Trees
summary: JFTA-Bench introduces a benchmark with 3,130 dialogue entries for evaluating how well LLMs can analyze system malfunctions using fault trees. The dataset includes complex scenarios with user errors and recovery procedures to test integrated task-tracking capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.22978v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In the maintenance of complex systems, fault trees are used to locate problems and provide targeted solutions. To enable fault trees stored as images to be directly processed by large language models, which can assist in tracking and analyzing malfunctions, we propose a novel textual representation of fault trees. Building on it, we construct a benchmark for multi-turn dialogue systems that emphasizes robust interaction in complex environments, evaluating a model's ability to assist in malfunction localization, which contains $3130$ entries and $40.75$ turns per entry on average. We train an end-to-end model to generate vague information to reflect user behavior and introduce long-range rollback and recovery procedures to simulate user error scenarios, enabling assessment of a model's integrated capabilities in task tracking and error recovery, and Gemini 2.5 pro archives the best performance.

zh.title: JFTA-Bench：评估LLM使用故障树追踪和分析故障的能力
zh.summary: JFTA-Bench引入了一个包含3,130个对话条目的基准，用于评估LLM使用故障树分析系统故障的能力。该数据集包括具有用户错误和恢复程序的复杂场景，以测试集成任务追踪能力。

fr.title: JFTA-Bench : Évaluer la capacité du LLM à suivre et analyser les dysfonctionnements à l'aide d'arbres de défaillance
fr.summary: JFTA-Bench introduit un benchmark avec 3 130 entrées de dialogue pour évaluer la capacité des LLM à analyser les dysfonctionnements du système à l'aide d'arbres de défaillance. L'ensemble de données comprend des scénarios complexes avec des erreurs utilisateur et des procédures de récupération pour tester les capacités intégrées de suivi des tâches.

de.title: JFTA-Bench: Bewertung der Fähigkeit von LLM zur Verfolgung und Analyse von Ausfällen mithilfe von Fehlerbäumen
de.summary: JFTA-Bench führt eine Benchmark mit 3.130 Dialogeinträgen ein, um zu bewerten, wie gut LLMs Systemausfälle mithilfe von Fehlerbäumen analysieren können. Der Datensatz umfasst komplexe Szenarien mit Benutzerfehlern und Wiederherstellungsverfahren, um integrierte Task-Tracking-Fähigkeiten zu testen.

es.title: JFTA-Bench: Evaluar la capacidad del LLM para rastrear y analizar malfuncionamientos usando árboles de fallos
es.summary: JFTA-Bench introduce un punto de referencia con 3.130 entradas de diálogo para evaluar qué tan bien los LLM pueden analizar malfuncionamientos del sistema usando árboles de fallos. El conjunto de datos incluye escenarios complejos con errores del usuario y procedimientos de recuperación para probar capacidades integradas de seguimiento de tareas.
